sessions.next: Designed to Disappear: Why Developers are Choosing the Even Realities G2 (Registration & Acceptance Required)

sessions.title: Designed to Disappear: Why Developers are Choosing the Even Realities G2 (Registration & Acceptance Required)
sessions.speaker: Tom Ouyang - Even Realities; Raag Harshavat - Even Realities
sessions.description: We're bringing G2 to AWE Long Beach for a workshop with the people who actually built it. ​Even G2 is the first pair of consumer smart glasses with a 576×288 monochrome waveguide display, the Even R1 ring as a dedicated input device, and an open plugin platform (Even Hub) running on real hardware in the wild today. ​This event is for the engineers, hardware folks, and platform-curious developers who want to go past the keynote slides and into the how — how the optics work, how the chip stack is designed, how the SDK exposes hardware to third-party plugins, and why we think glasses are the next computing platform. ​Most of the evening is conversation with Tom (CTO) and Raag (DevRel). The last block is hands-on: write a small plugin on a real pair of Even G2 so you walk out having actually shipped something to a pair of glasses. Register here: https://luma.com/p5ccgjxd Organizers will let you know if you've been accepted or added to a waitlist.

sessions.title: Android XR: High-Performance Development Across Engines and Form Factors (Registration & Acceptance Required)
sessions.speaker: Luke Hopkins - Google | Android XR
sessions.description: This workshop is designed to showcase the latest features for Android XR, focusing on tools that prioritize speed and developer iteration. We begin with a live demonstration of the Android XR Engine Hub, where we'll show how to manage the automatic installation of the XR Streaming Client APK directly to your headset, enabling near-instant code changes and live previews. Following this, we will move into a live coding session to build an Android XR experience from the ground up using Unity. We will demonstrate how a single codebase intelligently scales across the ecosystem, ensuring your project works seamlessly across both high-immersion headsets like Galaxy XR and lightweight wired glasses like Project Aura. To close, we will host partners who are shipping at the cutting edge. They will share their first-hand experience developing for Project Aura and Galaxy XR, demonstrating how a single Android XR codebase allowed them to target both high-immersion headsets and wired glasses without fragmentation overhead. Register here: https://forms.gle/84cSRoPYGsGBnCGD6 Organizers will let you know if you've been accepted or added to the waitlist.

sessions.title: Digital Humans in Production: Avatar SDK & HTC Viverse
sessions.speaker: Victor Erukhimov - Itseez3D; Andranik Aslanyan - HTC
sessions.description: Avatar SDK is a leader in generating game-ready avatars from images. sessions.next: Why Some XR Pitches Land — and Others Don’t: Lessons from Reality Hack at MIT

sessions.title: Why Some XR Pitches Land — and Others Don’t: Lessons from Reality Hack at MIT
sessions.speaker: Karen Lam - Reality Hack, Inc.; Haseeb Musa - Reality Hack, Inc.; Maria Rice - Reality Hack, Inc.
sessions.description: Over the past three years, Reality Hack at MIT Founders Lab has worked with XR creators trying to turn prototypes into startups. Some pitches stood out immediately; others struggled to make the leap from ideation and demo to investable opportunity. In this session, we’ll share the most important lessons we’ve seen from both successful and unsuccessful pitches, and what today’s VR creators can learn from them. Drawing from our experience mentoring award-winning teams and supporting founder development, we’ll unpack the common patterns that help builders better communicate customer need, market relevance, product positioning, and startup potential. Each January, founders in the program pitch to mentors that include investors and VCs in the MIT ecosystem, giving teams direct exposure to the questions and signals early-stage backers care about most. Learning Agenda: What separates a compelling XR founder pitch from a strong demo that still misses the mark The most common mistakes VR creators make when pitching to judges, mentors, investors, and partners How to better frame customer need, market opportunity, and product positioning What award-winning teams did differently to stand out

sessions.next: Creating Immersive Civic Experiences: How 360° VR is Transforming Public Engagement

sessions.title: Creating Immersive Civic Experiences: How 360° VR is Transforming Public Engagement
sessions.speaker: Marbenn Cayetano - City of Austin
sessions.description: Cities are increasingly exploring immersive technologies to connect with residents in meaningful ways. This session will showcase how the City of Austin has leveraged 360° Virtual Reality (VR) to enhance public engagement, cultural preservation, and accessibility. From virtual cave tours that educate residents about water conservation to an innovative Oakwood Cemetery 360° VR experience that contributed to a National Endowment for the Humanities grant, and a Municipal Court 360° VR tour recognized with the “Friends of the Court” award, these projects demonstrate the power of immersive storytelling in civic spaces. Attendees will learn about the challenges and opportunities of implementing VR in government, strategies for collaboration across departments and community partners, and lessons learned from award-winning projects. The session will also explore how immersive technology can foster transparency, education, and cultural connection—paving the way for future XR applications in public service.
sessions.next: Beyond the Gym: Building an Adaptive Wellness Ecosystem

sessions.title: Beyond the Gym: Building an Adaptive Wellness Ecosystem
sessions.speaker: Kathleen Cohen - The Collaboratorium; Jaime Pichardo García - Odders Lab; Sam Cole - FitXR
sessions.description: Millions of workouts in VR have taught us that the future of fitness isn't defined by a single device. It's defined by the intelligence behind it. Sam Cole (FitXR) and Jaime Pichardo García (Odders Lab) have collectively built some of the most-used fitness experiences in spatial computing. In this session, they'll share what they've learned and where it's all heading. From real-time adaptive coaching and emotionally responsive programming to biometric feedback loops across spatial computing, mobile, and wearable AI, the next generation of fitness isn't a gym. It's an ecosystem that learns, adapts, and meets people wherever they are.
sessions.next: Build Spatial Web with WebSpatial and AI accelerated Coding

sessions.title: Build Spatial Web with WebSpatial and AI accelerated Coding
sessions.speaker: Pratik Thakare - PICO; Ruoya Sheng - PICO
sessions.description: Tired of the flat web? The era of spatial computing is here, but building immersive apps often means leaving your web skills behind. Not anymore. This session introduces Webspatial, a new paradigm that empowers web developers to expand their craft from 2D to 3D. Learn how to break your pages out of their frames, elevating standard HTML elements into volumetric UI that lives in space. We’ll show you how to compose complex scenes with both 2D and 3D content for native-like performance, all while using the web technologies you already know. You’ll also see how AI-powered “vibe coding” lets you spin up Webspatial experiences by describing what you want to an agent, then refine the result with your own code. Leave this talk ready to build the next generation of spatial experiences and expand your web from 2D to true 3D reality.
sessions.next: Build VR Like a Theme Park. Market It Like a Movie.

sessions.title: Build VR Like a Theme Park. Market It Like a Movie.
sessions.speaker: Mike Wozniewski - Hololabs; Brett Gaylor - Hololabs
sessions.description: Theme parks spend billions designing experiences that people travel across the world to repeat. That level of craft has always been out of reach for independent VR operators — until now. Hololabs worked with Universal Studios on the Augmented Reality gameplay for Mario Kart: Bowser's Challenge now running in Osaka, Hollywood and Epic Universe. Then they asked a harder question: what would it look like to bring that same design philosophy to the arcade market? The answer was Abyss Vault — a 30-minute free-roam VR experience for 2–6 players built on the principles that make theme park attractions unforgettable: rich storytelling, stunning environments, and gameplay that demands real teamwork. Unusually for a single title, it was designed to hit three of the four audience segments that drive LBE revenue — birthday parties, active gamers, and corporate groups — bringing new audiences into venues that are hungry for a compelling, social experience. But building it was only half the challenge. Hololabs also bet on a different kind of launch: a coordinated worldwide premiere event, venue partners activated simultaneously, with the energy of a film release rather than a typical software rollout. In this session, the Hololabs team goes behind the scenes on both bets — the design process and the go-to-market — and shares what they learned from bringing the full playbook to market. Whether you're an operator evaluating our next title or a studio thinking about how to build and launch at this level, this is the blueprint.

sessions.next: From Headsets to Human-Scale Systems: What We Now Mean by XR

sessions.title: From Headsets to Human-Scale Systems: What We Now Mean by XR
sessions.speaker: Elizabeth Hyman - XR Association; Mark Sage - The AREA; Priyanka Shekar - HP; Evan Pesses - Verizon
sessions.description: For years, XR has been the shorthand for the headsets and hardware used in AR, VR, and MR. But as AI increasingly merges with immersive technology, that definition no longer reflects the industry’s direction. Rather than shrinking, XR is maturing, moving into workplaces and critical sectors as a human-scale system – that senses context, interprets it with AI, and delivers spatial responses in real time. Immersive headsets remain essential when deep presence drives outcomes, but they now operate within a broader stack that includes wearables, digital twins, and edge intelligence. As the industry deploys at scale, how we define XR is more than just semantic. It's strategic. This conversation examines what maturity looks like when measurable impact, integration, and long-term infrastructure define success in an AI-driven world.

sessions.next: Roundtable Discussions: XR in Location Based Entertainment: From Experiment to Ecosystem

sessions.title: Roundtable Discussions: XR in Location Based Entertainment: From Experiment to Ecosystem
sessions.speaker: Raymond Mosco - General Immersive; Grant Hinkson - Parietal Lab; Julian Triveri - Anaglyph
sessions.description: Location based entertainment is becoming one of the most commercially viable and creatively ambitious sectors in XR. This roundtable brings together enthusiasts and leaders in immersive attractions, spatial computing, multiplayer systems, and experiential design to discuss how XR is reshaping entertainment venues, retail destinations, museums, and live events. Round table guests and attendees will explore advances in AR glasses, free-roam VR, mixed reality interaction, digital twins, AI-driven experiences, and persistent spatial content — along with the operational realities of deploying XR at scale. Designed for an audience of XR builders, platform providers, and innovative storytellers, this session examines where immersive entertainment is heading next. We invite you to come with questions and leave with inspiration, innovation, connections, and related highlights of the AWE conference to check out throughout the week.
sessions.next: VR in the Lab: How Digital Worlds Reveal the Science of Memory

sessions.title: VR in the Lab: How Digital Worlds Reveal the Science of Memory
sessions.speaker: Rolando Masís-Obando - Johns Hopkins University
sessions.description: Our memories are shaped by the places we visit. Every experience happens somewhere and in virtual reality, experiences can happen anywhere. In this talk, I’ll show how immersive VR environments are used as scientific tools to study how the brain forms and recalls memories, revealing how space, context, and interaction shape what we remember. We’ll talk about what makes an environment memorable and what neuroscience tells us about the future of immersive design.

sessions.next: XR in Action: Making STEM Visible, Empowering, and Achievable

sessions.title: XR in Action: Making STEM Visible, Empowering, and Achievable
sessions.speaker: Sydney Hamilton - The Boeing Company
sessions.description: For many students, especially girls and those from underrepresented communities, STEM can feel out of reach — until they see themselves in it. Extended Reality (XR) has the power to change that by making learning immersive, engaging, and personal. In this session, aerospace leader and IF/THEN Ambassador Sydney Hamilton demonstrates how XR can transform STEM education. Drawing on her experience leading groundbreaking aerospace projects — from sustainable aircraft development to satellite engineering — Sydney shows how immersive simulations, virtual labs, and interactive experiences help students visualize themselves as innovators, problem-solvers, and creators. Attendees will discover: How XR bridges gaps in access and representation, making STEM more empowering for all students. Real-world applications of XR in classrooms, mentorship programs, and community initiatives. Strategies for educators, creators, and organizations to design experiences that inspire curiosity, confidence, and long-term engagement in STEM. This talk highlights XR’s potential not just as a tool for learning, but as a catalyst for empowerment, opportunity, and the next generation of innovators.
sessions.next: The Sound of Smart Glasses: Bringing Voice AI into the Real World

sessions.title: The Sound of Smart Glasses: Bringing Voice AI into the Real World
sessions.speaker: Nik Jedrzejewski - NXP Semiconductors; Laurent Pilati - NXP Semiconductors
sessions.description: AI is moving beyond screens and into the real world creating more natural, contextual and immersive ways for humans to interact with technology. Smart glasses are emerging as a key platform for this shift, blending voice, audio and edge AI into experiences that feel seamless, responsive and always available. Join NXP as we explore the technologies shaping the future of wearable AI. From beamforming and wake word detection to low-power, real-time audio processing on i.MX RT crossover MCUs, this keynote will examine the engineering challenges and innovations driving next-generation smart glasses.
sessions.next: The Immersive Supervisor: A Role Hollywood Didn't Know It Needed

sessions.title: The Immersive Supervisor: A Role Hollywood Didn't Know It Needed
sessions.speaker: Matthew Celia - Light Sail VR
sessions.description: This talk makes the case that immersive productions fail or succeed based on a role that didn't exist five years ago: the person who bridges traditional film crews and immersive capture, managing camera placement, real-time monitoring, and preventing the kind of on-set mistakes that become unfixable in post. Using case studies from the SNL 50th (coordinating with a live broadcast), The Faceless Lady (narrative VR with Eli Roth), and multicamera concert capture, Co-Founder and Creative Director Matthew Celia defines what this role actually is, why it matters, and what it means for the professionalization of immersive production.
sessions.next: How Continuous Hardware Innovation Drives ROI by Reducing Total Cost of Ownership

sessions.title: How Continuous Hardware Innovation Drives ROI by Reducing Total Cost of Ownership
sessions.speaker: Dax Leepart - PICO
sessions.description: Sustainability in XR isn't just about the environment; it’s about business endurance. This session demonstrates how PICO’s commitment to continuous hardware innovation and enterprise-grade support directly lowers TCO, ensuring that your XR investment delivers long-term value through a predictable and high-performance roadmap.
sessions.next: Building for the Android XR Ecosystem using Unity, Unreal, and Godot

sessions.title: Building for the Android XR Ecosystem using Unity, Unreal, and Godot
sessions.speaker: Luke Hopkins - Google | Android XR
sessions.description: Go beyond the basics and learn how to build high-performance, immersive experiences using the industry’s leading game engines. This session explores the comprehensive support for Unity, Unreal Engine, and Godot on Android XR, including deep integration with native AndroidXR vendor extensions. We will break down the three essential pillars of our developer stack: the AndroidXR Interaction Toolkit for seamless input handling, the Android XR Audio SDK for professional-grade spatial sound, and the Android XR Engine Hub for real-time debugging. Join us to see how these tools work in harmony to streamline your workflow and elevate the quality of your XR content.
sessions.next: The XR Stage Advantage: Reimaging Collaboration in Teamwork Simulations

sessions.title: The XR Stage Advantage: Reimaging Collaboration in Teamwork Simulations
sessions.speaker: Michelle Aebersold - University of Michigan School of Nursing
sessions.description: Creating a teamwork simulation leveraging virtual production stage (VPS) technology—a blend of giant LED walls and real-time 3D rendering (like Unreal Engine) allowed our team to create a highly scalable immersive training environment for healthcare teams. Creating a choose your own adventure style learning simulation that can engage teams of health professionals was a collaborative effort by nursing faculty, XR developers, media specialists, and others. The final product produced a simulation that feels more authentic than traditional 2D video. Our presentation showcases how XR developers can design and implement branching, team-focused case simulations using VPS. This unlocks new learning opportunities: improved teamwork, communication, and leadership skills—all in a setting that’s easily scalable for classroom or remote use. We’ll demo our “Navigating Team Dynamics” simulation, share workflow tips, and discuss evaluation methods. Discover how XR virtual stages can revolutionize collaborative training for high-stakes environments, while driving better outcomes for both teams and users.

sessions.next: Bystander Signaling in the Era of AI Devices

sessions.title: Bystander Signaling in the Era of AI Devices
sessions.speaker: Nathan White - Meta
sessions.description: Since the Kodak Brownie made photography portable over a century ago, society has grappled with social acceptance. As AI devices evolve to incorporate multimodal AI and contextual processing, the industry must combine existing precedent with novel approaches to meet the needs of new technology and use cases. This session details the framework Meta has developed—informed by research, public input, and policymaker engagement—for thinking about how to signal to bystanders when capture, environmental processing, or AI features are active on wearable devices. While expectations in this space continue to evolve, the presentation shares key social and technological factors Meta has considered and calls for a common industry approach to social acceptance as these technologies grow.
sessions.next: Scaling the Personal AI Ecosystem

sessions.title: Scaling the Personal AI Ecosystem
sessions.speaker: Nima Shams - Qualcomm
sessions.description: As personal AI begins to move beyond phones and displays and into the things we wear every day, the path from idea to product is becoming increasingly complex. In this AWE session, Qualcomm will explore a new way of thinking about how intelligent wearables and emerging personal AI devices come to life. Learn more about Qualcomm's efforts to scale the smart glasses ecosystem.

sessions.title: Presence Is the New Premium: Immersive Video Survives the AI Flood!
sessions.speaker: Hugh Hou - Metaverse Stage; Keeley Turner - Metaverse Stage
sessions.description: Generative AI is collapsing the value of flat video and synthetic 3D game-engine media. Audiences want and crave the opposite: real places, real people, real moments that they can actually trust are real. Immersive video is one format that AI can't fake! Stereoscopic 8K-per-eye capture at 90fps, anchors the audience in a real place and time, delivering something diffusion models still can't touch: presence. That's why the Lakers Apple Immersive livestream felt so visceral on Vision Pro. Immersive filmmakers have the unique ability to engage viewers through emotion, sight, and sound to simulate the feeling of presence. Hugh Hou and Keeley Turner, filmmakers behind Spatial Film, break down their Apple Immersive Video work on Henry VIII in Warwick, Aspen Cowgirl, and the Vanilla Lily Series with the Blackmagic URSA Cine Immersive and how XR creators can build careers in 2026 that survive the next platform collapse.
sessions.next: 290 Million Minutes of XR: The Signals That Determine What Scales and What Stalls

sessions.title: 290 Million Minutes of XR: The Signals That Determine What Scales and What Stalls
sessions.speaker: Nicola Anderson - Cognitive3D
sessions.description: How spatial behavior data turns XR quality from a gut feeling into a number you can hold a deployment to. Why do two XR deployments with the same hardware, same rollout, and same organization end up worlds apart on adoption? We'll discuss why, in enterprise, quality behaves less like a spectrum and more like a threshold you're either above or below. Drawing on patterns observed across more than 290 million minutes of real spatial behavior data spanning enterprise training, healthcare, social, fitness, and games, this talk unpacks what actually determines whether an XR experience succeeds or stalls. We'll look at how comfort, onboarding, and spatial friction can be measured directly from user behavior. Every pattern is grounded in real data, not anecdote, with practical implications for how teams set quality baselines, hold a live deployment to them, and catch a struggling rollout in week one instead of quarter two. You'll leave able to treat XR quality as something you measure, not something you hope for.
sessions.next: XR for Decision-Making in Sports and Health

sessions.title: XR for Decision-Making in Sports and Health
sessions.speaker: Habib Noorbhai - North-West University, South Africa
sessions.description: In elite sport, a race can be decided by 40-90 milliseconds. In surgery, 100 milliseconds can determine precision. In aviation, photography, emergency response and everyday life, our actions are shaped by decisions made faster than conscious thought. Drawing on research spanning sports science, performance psychology and healthcare training, this talk explores how XR environments can influence metacognitive processes such as self-regulation, awareness and decision-making (with specific examples on batting sports, where lab data shows XR measurably alters movement mechanics). As spatial computing moves mainstream, the question is no longer whether XR will change how we see digital content, but how it will shape human behaviour itself.

Without appropriate safeguards, sensitive data can be exposed through vulnerabilities in visual displays, audio capture, data management, and security protocols. The integration of AI into XR healthcare applications exponentially increases both the capability and vulnerability of these systems. This panel discussion will address critical considerations for clinicians, IT personnel, and others implementing XR programs. Attendees will gain practical insights into building privacy frameworks that protect patients while building trust. sessions.title: Decoding Reality: Schizophrenia, Perception & Equitable XR Design
sessions.speaker: Kat King - CatheXis Films
sessions.description: Schizophrenia affects about 24 million people worldwide and 1 in 100 U.S. adults. Among veterans, the crisis is even more acute. In 2017, approximately 127,445 Veterans Health Administration patients were diagnosed with schizophrenia—nearly double the rate in the general population. For those with schizophrenia, the danger often lies in stats you don't usually hear about. For example, they are 10–14 times more likely to be victims of violence than to pose any threat themselves. These aren’t just percentages—they’re real people under siege. Over 127,000 U.S. veterans with schizophrenia face heightened vulnerability. Millions more live with psychosis, marginalization, and daily threat. And yet the systems meant to support them ignore their reality. This session isn’t about profit—it’s about survival and equity. As a disabled Army veteran designing for treatment-resistant schizophrenia, Kat King has built PerceptAR—an AR tool that grounds users during hallucinations and dissociation, using real-time environmental overlays and cognitive prompts. In this session she’ll explore: Trust and alignment in XR/AI for historically harmed communities Trauma-informed design for psychosis support The ethics of building with—not for—neurodivergent users Why disabled and veteran-led leadership is essential in mental health XR This is not speculative. It’s urgent, human-centered survival design—technology built to keep people alive, not to cash in.

sessions.speaker: Piotr Krukowski - Inseye Inc.
sessions.description: AI glasses are becoming multimodal systems. What is still missing is a low-power signal of the user’s state. Inseye Tiny is designed to provide that signal through eye-movement sensing. By detecting activity related patterns, Tiny can help multimodal AI glasses become more personal and less interruptive. This talk will focus on the system opportunity: how eye-movement signals can shape both AI behavior and HUD behavior. Instead of always responding the same way, glasses can learn when to wait, when to nudge, when to unfold a notification, when to trigger OCR, when to scroll, and when to ask for confirmation. Tiny is designed for real eyewear constraints, enabling user context and interface control without full camera-level gaze tracking.

From hands-on studies of motor coordination and cognitive load to practical models for university–industry partnerships, this conversation highlights how data-driven approaches to esports can help: Create research-backed methods for improving focus, strategy, and collaboration skills across learner populations. Foster interdisciplinary connections across games research, neuroscience, and education. Increase participation from underrepresented groups through accessible team formats and mentoring models. Accessible AI and XR can expand talent pools and support onboarding, upskilling in skilled trades and technical qualification assessments, and even onsite assistance for workers with and without disabilities. sessions.next: X-RAI Vision: Bio-Interactive XR for Objective Functional Assessment

sessions.title: X-RAI Vision: Bio-Interactive XR for Objective Functional Assessment
sessions.speaker: Nathan Miner - Massachusetts General Hospital
sessions.description: Musculoskeletal conditions require adaptive reorganization of the body’s movement to compensate for pain and functional limitations. These movement changes manifest as patterns that carry diagnostic information that no standard clinical measure can currently capture. This talk introduces bio-interactive XR as a research tool that integrates wearable sensors and machine learning to investigate the body's adaptive intelligence as a new paradigm for objective functional assessment. Drawing on our foundational studies, this talk covers: The measurement gap: Why current clinical outcome measures fail to capture what matters most about how patients actually function. Variability as signal: How patient movement data captured in bio interactive systems contains diagnostic patterns invisible to standard observation. From assessment to intervention: How the same bio-interactive paradigm extends from diagnostic applications to therapeutic delivery. Attendees will leave with a concrete understanding of how XR and wearable sensor integration can advance objective clinical measurement, practical insights into the design challenges of building assessment systems for healthcare, and an understanding of what’s next.

sessions.title: From Web Browser to Metaverse Browser
sessions.speaker: Sean Mann - RP1; Neil Trevett - Metaverse Standards Forum
sessions.description: The open-standards moment for AR glasses and the spatial services they need The web scaled on a simple premise: anyone can host content, any standards-based client can display it, and no single company controls either side. Spatial computing now faces the same choice. You can put on AR glasses today, but the current web stack cannot browse geolocated spatial content. Either we build an open, standards-based web for spatial content and services — or we risk staying locked into proprietary walled gardens. In this session, Sean Mann (CEO, RP1) and Neil Trevett (President, Metaverse Standards Forum and Khronos Group) will pull back the curtain on the Open Metaverse Browser Initiative and Sneeze — the first open-source metaverse browser engine, hosted at the MSF to invite broad participation from standards bodies and industry. Leave knowing exactly what is being built, who is building it, and how to get involved.
sessions.next: Meet the Sparse Processing Unit (SPU) chip and AI developer platform: Unlocking the Next Generation of Smart Glasses

sessions.title: Meet the Sparse Processing Unit (SPU) chip and AI developer platform: Unlocking the Next Generation of Smart Glasses
sessions.speaker: Bill Hoppin - femtoAI
sessions.description: Today's smart glasses have failed to achieve mass adoption due to three key problems: unattractive bulky designs, poor battery life, and unreliable voice interfaces with low audio quality. The Sparse Processing Unit (SPU) chip and AI developer platform addresses all three. It enables smart glasses that are light and stylish, last all day on a single charge, deliver crystal-clear audio, and unlocks a voice interface that works reliably — even in noisy environments or at low speech volumes. The SPU achieves this through ultra-low power consumption and latency, making larger on-device AI models practical for the first time. With 10x model compression efficiency, the SPU delivers outstanding performance at 100x lower power within existing memory budgets — unlocking use cases previously out of reach. This talk provides an overview of the SPU chip and developer platform and the unique capabilities it brings to smart glasses.

In this talk, hear from the companies driving the format forward—across hardware, production, and narrative craft—as they share the developments, emerging best practices, and a glimpse of where immersive video is headed next.
sessions.next: PANEL: Gaussian Splat Workflow – From Data Collection to Display

sessions.title: PANEL: Gaussian Splat Workflow – From Data Collection to Display
sessions.speaker: Sandy Ressler - Khronos Group; Tamrat Belayneh - ESRI; Mindy Li - XGRIDS; Michael Rubloff - Radiance Fields
sessions.description: Gaussian splatting is emerging as a powerful production tool for creating static and dynamic, large-scale, photorealistic 3D assets, but many teams struggle to move from reconstruction to deployment. This panel provides XR developers and content creators with practical guidance on integrating Gaussian splats into production workflows, from data acquisition and reconstruction through optimization and delivery. Panelists will share real-world use cases, tooling insights, and performance best practices, with a focus on using the glTF asset format as a standardized delivery mechanism for web and native, cross-platform 3D applications. The session will also explore new and emerging capabilities for representing and rendering Gaussian splats within glTF-based pipelines, including approaches to lighting, interaction, and future extensibility.
sessions.next: Create Knowledge that Sticks: 5 Golden Rules behind Triumphant Immersive Learning Programs

sessions.title: Create Knowledge that Sticks: 5 Golden Rules behind Triumphant Immersive Learning Programs
sessions.speaker: Angus Stevens - Start Beyond
sessions.description: Everyone wants their training to be memorable but how do you do it? To create knowledge, start with the heart. Because emotions create memories, and it’s those memories that uplift training into knowledge and experience. While the effectiveness of VR when compared to traditional learning is well documented, and the number of workforces deploying VR & AR solutions is growing, the question remains, how does it relate to your business? When should you use it? And given not all training can be driven by feelings, how can an emotional connection in VR, be used to address such topics as, Workplace Health & Safety regulatory requirements? In this session Angus Stevens, CEO of Australia’s leading immersive learning studio, will unlock the 5 guiding principles behind building unforgettable VR and AR training programs, articulate the critical questions that determine whether it’s the right solution for your business, and breakdown the challenges, costs, and distribution strategies that guarantee success.
sessions.next: Eye-Controlled Ambient Computing for Prosumers and Enterprise

sessions.title: Eye-Controlled Ambient Computing for Prosumers and Enterprise
sessions.speaker: Thomas Suarez - Raven Resonance; Parth Arora - Raven Resonance
sessions.description: Raven Resonance has created the first wearable Ambient Computer - the future of safety and efficiency. Raven hardware and software allow people to see contextual information while controlling applications hands-free. Privacy-preserving eye control, 64-bit quad-core ARM compute, and a full-color LCoS waveguide display are delivered in the form factor of lightweight, stylish glasses for all-day wear. Hot-swappable batteries eliminate power interruptions. Raven’s open Linux-based OS allows deployment of proprietary and open-source software, built with the Raven SDK (typical initial app development time: 20 minutes) or any existing application that runs on Linux. The Raven team helped pioneer wearable computing and is here at AWE with a sneak preview of what's next for both prosumers and enterprise.
sessions.next: PANEL: XR and AI at Work: From Real-Time Guidance to Edge Deployment

sessions.title: PANEL: XR and AI at Work: From Real-Time Guidance to Edge Deployment
sessions.speaker: Logan Lawler - Dell Technologies; Greg Barbone - NVIDIA; Lisa Bell-Cabrera - NVIDIA; Le Cong - Stanford University | AutoBio; Jacob Biehl - University of Pittsburgh, School of Computing and Information
sessions.description: XR and generative AI are coming together to deliver real-time guidance in the flow of work. This Dell Technologies and NVIDIA panel will explore how AI-powered XR can help users interpret data, understand physical environments, and access expert support hands-free, especially where speed, privacy, and local compute matter. Healthcare and life sciences will serve as examples, including AI-enabled lab workflows from Stanford that could accelerate life-saving therapies, and augmented reality surgical guidance from the University of Pittsburgh/UPMC designed to help surgeons work faster and smarter. The discussion will also highlight how leading university labs are becoming launchpads for commercialization.
sessions.next: The Missing Link in Smart Glasses

sessions.title: The Missing Link in Smart Glasses
sessions.speaker: Paul Meissner - Applied Materials
sessions.description: For years, ubiquitous augmented reality has felt within reach – yet always just out of grasp. Brighter displays come at the cost of efficiency. More powerful systems compromise wearability. Breakthroughs impress in demos but struggle to scale. What if the challenge isn’t any single technology – but how they all come together? In this session, Paul Meissner from Applied Materials explores the hidden constraint that has shaped the category’s progress – and what happens when performance, design, and manufacturability are approached as one system from the start. The result is a path forward for smart glasses – a path that is already here.

sessions.next: Create Social Stories: LBE Marketing & Community-Building

sessions.title: Create Social Stories: LBE Marketing & Community-Building
sessions.speaker: Ryan Bromsgrove - vrCAVE / Basement Bunker Labs; Ashley Blake - vrCAVE
sessions.description: The big question in LBE is "how do you market VR"? It's a medium that defies many typical marketing strategies. Players can't take photos or video, and they're engaged in an activity only they can see. Building communities within the LBE area also has unique challenges, but there are ways to connect players and create global LBE communities centered around particular games or demographics. Learn how to create positive practices from the beginning. Build a community that will organically become part of your marketing strategy to help generate the revenue you need to succeed.
sessions.next: Designing and Building Enjoyable Immersive Worlds

sessions.title: Designing and Building Enjoyable Immersive Worlds
sessions.speaker: Steve Tagger - nDreams Ltd; Lucas Martell - Mighty Coconut; Sonya Haskins - AWE
sessions.description: Join us for a fireside chat with executives from two top VR gaming studios as they discuss their experiences designing and building new immersive worlds. Where do they get their ideas and at what point do they know this is a world they want to design? How do they work with their teams to make those worlds "real" (in the immersive environment)? Do common physical limitations (motion sickness, mobility, etc.) affect their design choices and if so, how? Or, does VR allow greater freedom from physical limitation? How do they make choices related to embodied interaction? How important is environmental storytelling - the players' experience, lore, etc. - to the overall design process? Are they using AI in their design / world-building process? How do you determine the best options for UX/UI for a particular world?

sessions.title: Roundtable Discussions: Open Standards - Web Wide Worlds
sessions.speaker: Dylan Baker - Five Squared Interactive
sessions.description: Join us in discussing how Web Wide Worlds can make worldbuilding approachable for everyone: from hobbyists to studios, by using the open Web as a foundation. We’ll look at examples from gaming, digital twins, research, and generative AI, including ways to build on existing creations like Minecraft worlds and Cities: Skylines creations. We encourage attendees to bring questions and share their ideas; this is an open discussion about what becomes possible when the Web itself becomes a place for worlds!
sessions.next: Training the Heart of Healthcare with VR and AI

sessions.title: Training the Heart of Healthcare with VR and AI
sessions.speaker: Isabela Moise - Aequilibrium; Adrian Moise - Aequilibrium
sessions.description: Healthcare systems around the world share one ultimate goal: elevating patient care. Achieving that goal requires clinicians who are not only clinically competent but also empathetic, resilient, and effective communicators, which are skills that directly shape safety, satisfaction, and outcomes. Yet these affective competencies are some of the most difficult to teach, sustain, and scale. While the challenge begins with struggling to find the resources and capabilities to standardize med student preparation, it persist in clinics and hospital with overwhelmed healthcare workers taken off the floor for hours at a time for ineffective training. Fortunately, AI-powered VR training is emerging as a transformative solution. In this session, we will share insights from Aequilibrium’s co-leadership of VR4Health, a project involving a global consortium of partners dedicated to advancing immersive clinician education starting in med schools and extending all the way to re-skilling veteran healthcare workers. Together, we are building VR experiences driven by AI to offer personalized ‘just-in-time’ learning designed to strengthen the emotional, cognitive and psychomotor skills that most impact clinical quality and thus patient care. We will explore subjects like emotionally responsive virtual patients that can help evaluate student diagnostic and bedside capabilities, and how to develop proficiency that enables active recall in high-acuity scenarios like code whites while improving teamwork and reducing errors. By supporting continuous upskilling and emotional readiness, these solutions directly build more resilient care teams and meaningfully improve patient care.
sessions.next: From Hype to Healing: Proving Mental Wellness Outcomes with the Multi-Modal AI Stack

sessions.title: From Hype to Healing: Proving Mental Wellness Outcomes with the Multi-Modal AI Stack
sessions.speaker: Nanea Reeves - TRIPP
sessions.description: Mental health is one of the defining challenges of our time, and immersive technology has promised solutions for years. But beautiful environments and conversational AI are not enough. Real impact requires measurable outcomes. In this session, TRIPP CEO Nanea Reeves shares what it takes to move from promise to proof. Drawing on real-world data from millions of sessions across mobile, AI smartglasses, and mixed reality, she explains why the device is not the differentiator. The modality stack is. The future of digital mental wellness will not be defined by headsets or apps alone. It will be defined by adaptive, multi-modal AI systems that integrate audio, visual, biometric, and behavioral signals and intelligently orchestrate them across contexts. Whether someone is on the subway, at their desk, or fully immersed in XR, the system must respond to their state and environment in real time. Nanea will break down: Why modality orchestration matters more than form factor What actually drives measurable shifts in calm, focus, and emotional regulation How mobile AI and spatial computing are converging into a scalable, outcome-driven wellness infrastructure This session reframes immersive AI not as an experience layer, but as a responsive and evidence-informed mental wellness platform that enterprise, healthcare leaders, and investors can no longer ignore.
sessions.next: The Met in VR: Why One of America’s Oldest Museums Is Investing in Immersive

sessions.title: The Met in VR: Why One of America’s Oldest Museums Is Investing in Immersive
sessions.speaker: Brett Renfer - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
sessions.description: Join us for a presentation of The Metropolitan Museum of Art's latest forays into VR/XR , challenges and successes, audience engagement, possible future activations, and more.
sessions.next: Engineering the Impossible: Photonics Platforms for High-Volume Manufacturing of Invisible AI Eyewear

sessions.title: Engineering the Impossible: Photonics Platforms for High-Volume Manufacturing of Invisible AI Eyewear
sessions.speaker: Ludovic Godet - Applied Materials
sessions.description: The next major inflection in computing and eyewear requires display technology that delivers premium augmented reality while remaining invisible to the wearer. This presentation introduces a human-centric visual system built on advanced nanophotonic waveguide platforms, engineered to bring AI-powered smart eyewear to high-volume manufacturing. The platform features a full-color, single-sheet waveguide display achieving 9,200 nits/lm brightness — rainbow-free with outstanding color uniformity — in all-day wearable glasses under 50 grams. Personalization through multiple frame shapes, lens tints, and integrated prescription correction addresses a key barrier to consumer adoption.
sessions.next: From Idea to Experience in Lens Studio

sessions.title: From Idea to Experience in Lens Studio
sessions.speaker: Luke Cartwright - SNAP
sessions.description: Go beyond vibe coding and explore a new way to build Lenses from end to end. In this practical workshop, Snap will walk through how developers can move from concept to prototype faster using new AI-powered workflows across design, prototyping, testing, debugging, and optimization. Attendees will see real examples, learn best practices, and get a clearer sense of how these new workflows can make Lens development more approachable, flexible, and efficient while leaving more room to focus on creative decisions and craft.

sessions.title: Building Content for Meta AI Glasses
sessions.speaker: Oscar Falmer - Meta; Abhijit Srikanth - Meta
sessions.description: Join us to explore the latest capabilities of Meta’s developer tools for building cutting-edge content on Meta AI glasses. We’ll also feature the best integrations created by our global community.

sessions.description: Select builders and creators who applied to participate in the Builders Program will have two minutes to present a quick "elevator pitch" to the audience. sessions.title: Roundtable Discussions: Open Standards - Godot Game Engine
sessions.speaker: David Snopek - W4 Games; Fredia Huya-Kouadio - Godot Engine
sessions.description: We'll discuss how the open source Godot Engine - with built-in support for open standards like OpenXR, WebXR, and glTF - enables developers to create interoperable XR experiences that they fully own and control. The conversation will draw on lessons from real-world Godot applications, both commercial and community-driven. Attendees are encouraged to bring questions about developing their own XR applications with open source tools and standards.
sessions.next: The Clinician’s Lens on Implementation: Bringing XR into Everyday Care

sessions.title: The Clinician’s Lens on Implementation: Bringing XR into Everyday Care
sessions.speaker: Emily Daviss - AVRwell Inc
sessions.description: Immersive technology is often discussed in terms of potential, but what does implementation really look like in practice? In this session, Emily Daviss, PT, DPT, Chief Clinical Officer and Co-Founder of AVRwell, brings the clinician’s lens to XR adoption, drawing from over a decade of direct patient care and national experience standardizing immersive healthcare across large hospital systems. She will share real-world lessons on utilization, scalability, and workflow integration, highlighting not only how XR can be implemented effectively, but also the barriers clinicians face and the creative strategies that make adoption successful. Attendees will gain practical strategies for introducing XR into everyday care, insights into driving clinician and patient engagement, and a deeper understanding of how immersive tools can move beyond pilots to achieve meaningful, system-wide impact. This is a conversation grounded in reality, focused on what actually works for patients, providers, and health systems.
sessions.next: The Light School: A Vision for Transcendence and Hope

sessions.title: The Light School: A Vision for Transcendence and Hope
sessions.speaker: Tom Furness - Virtual World Society - Light School
sessions.description: At AWE 2025, the Virtual World Society launched the Light School to promote personal transcendence and spread hope. The school views everyone as a "Soulship," able to explore realities with imagination, compassion, and love. Using XR, AI, and biofeedback, students engage in experiential learning for self-reflection and growth, integrating technology as a tool for harmony among body, mind, and spirit. Enlightenment is achieved through service to others. Since July 2025, a dedicated team has been developing its curriculum and technologies. Prof. Tom Furness will present the latest vision and advancements.
sessions.next: Accelerating Quest Development with Agentic Tools

sessions.title: Accelerating Quest Development with Agentic Tools
sessions.speaker: Dilmer Valecillos - Meta
sessions.description: How fast can you go from a game idea to a playable prototype on Quest? In this session, we’ll show how agentic AI workflows can help you move from concept to implementation using Meta MCP Extensions, Unity AI, AI Coding Assistants, and skills from our Agentic Tools GitHub repo. You’ll see how these tools can help plan your project, scaffold core systems, build features, and iterate quickly. We’ll also use Horizon Debug Bridge (hzdb), our CLI / MCP tool, to give agents better VR context from our docs and support rapid on-device testing, so you can validate changes without breaking your flow. Walk away with a practical, repeatable workflow for turning ideas into working builds faster than you thought possible.
sessions.next: Roundtable Discussions: Open Metaverse Browser Initiative

sessions.title: Roundtable Discussions: Open Metaverse Browser Initiative
sessions.speaker: Sean Mann - RP1; Neil Trevett - Metaverse Standards Forum
sessions.description: Architecture, roadmap, and how to get involved in the open spatial web Someone will build the metaverse browser. OMBI exists to make sure it's open. The choices being made today will define what the metaverse becomes — and at AWE 2026, that work is no longer theoretical. Sneeze, the open-source metaverse browser engine, is running in the first native metaverse browser. What gets built on top of it, and who shapes what comes next, is the conversation we're having now. Join Sean Mann (CEO, RP1) and Neil Trevett (President, Metaverse Standards Forum) for an open discussion on Sneeze's architecture, the open standards roadmap, how OMBI connects with standards and tools you already use, and how to get directly involved. Come with questions. The metaverse will be built by the people who show up.
sessions.next: AI-Powered Digital Twins: Real-World Workflows from VNTANA, NVIDIA & Astec Industries

sessions.title: AI-Powered Digital Twins: Real-World Workflows from VNTANA, NVIDIA & Astec Industries
sessions.speaker: Ashley Crowder - VNTANA; Chase Walker - Astec Industries
sessions.description: The Intelligent Metaverse is no longer a concept, it’s being built today at the intersection of AI and spatial computing. In this session, Ashley Crowder , CEO & Co-founder of VNTANA , and Chase Walker , Senior Simulation Engineer at Astec Industries , share how they partnered with NVIDIA to create automated CAD-to-Omniverse workflows that transform raw engineering data into AI-ready, simulation-accurate digital twins. Enabling the generation of simulated data to train AI for predictive maintenance, safety improvements, and operational efficiency in the field. Attendees will gain: Proven strategies for scaling CAD-to-Omniverse automation across enterprise environments Best practices for preparing 3D assets to feed AI models and digital twins Firsthand insights from Astec’s real-world implementation and measurable results A forward-looking view of how AI and spatial computing are converging to deliver ROI across industries Whether you’re an engineer, innovator, or digital transformation leader, this session offers a blueprint for turning 3D and AI into actionable intelligence today.
sessions.next: Snap Session

sessions.title: Snap Session
sessions.description: Coming soon!
sessions.next: Prescription on Demand

sessions.title: Prescription on Demand
sessions.speaker: Rob Stevens - Oxford Optical Labs
sessions.description: Frictionless correction of optical prescriptions is a key enabler for the adoption and market growth of XR. It is essential for providing a clear, comfortable XR experience to over half of the market, demonstrating at point-of-sale, enabling home sharing and enterprise applications, and maintaining resale value in the headset. Our solution provides a perfectly corrected experience within seconds using a fluid-lens technology that delivers full spherical and astigmatic correction in a compact, integrable module. The same technology also creates more immersive experiences by enabling variable depth of the focal plane. Come to our presentation to find out more.
sessions.next: Why Two Experienced Producers Made the Pivot from Traditional to Immersive Media

sessions.title: Why Two Experienced Producers Made the Pivot from Traditional to Immersive Media
sessions.speaker: Paul Mezier - Acute Immersive; Maura Axelrod - Primary: News in Depth; Kelly Breaks - Blue Frog Immersive
sessions.description: Hear first-hand from two experienced producers in traditional media who decided to invest heavily in immersive video. Maura Axelrod is an accomplished Associated Press reporter who launched “Primary: News in Depth”, the first news application on Apple Vision Pro leveraging Spatial Video to cover world events. Kelly Breaks is the owner of Blue Frog studios, a premier live music venue with a successful streaming business, who with his team of 12 is now recording multiple concerts every week with the Blackmagic URSA Cine Immersive camera. Learn about the story behind their decision to jump into Immersive, the challenges they faced and the solutions they found to overcome these. The fireside chat will be moderated by Paul Mezier, co-founder and COO of Acute Immersive, the company behind the publishing platform that powers both applications.
sessions.next: Augmenting Radiotherapy Planning through Spatial Interface and Human–AI Collaboration

sessions.title: Augmenting Radiotherapy Planning through Spatial Interface and Human–AI Collaboration
sessions.speaker: Alex Yang - Lixel Inc.
sessions.description: Radiotherapy planning is fundamentally a complex 3D spatial problem, yet clinicians still rely heavily on fragmented 2D interfaces such as CT slices and DVH charts. Radiation oncologists and medical physicists must mentally reconstruct spatial relationships between tumors, dose distributions, and critical organs, creating high cognitive burden and slower decision-making. To address this challenge, Lixel combines a glasses-free 3D floating display, mid-air interaction, AI segmentation and 3D reconstruction, and AI-integrated RTSS software. The platform transforms conventional treatment plans and DICOM data into intuitive 3D visualization and interaction, enabling clinicians to directly review and edit spatial dose information in 3D. Co-developed with Hualien Tzu Chi Hospital, the platform demonstrates the potential to improve spatial understanding, accelerate planning workflows, and enhance multidisciplinary collaboration. Beyond radiotherapy, Lixel believes this spatial interface approach can expand into broader healthcare and other high-stakes industries requiring complex 3D understanding and human–AI collaboration.
sessions.next: Built to Be Felt: Lessons from Shipping XR That Players Love

sessions.title: Built to Be Felt: Lessons from Shipping XR That Players Love
sessions.speaker: Liz Newton-Tanzer - Mighty Coconut; Inga Petryaevskaya - ShapesXR; Ethan Soo - techToy Studio; Saul Pena Gamero - Supernatural
sessions.description: A panel of engineers and founders behind some of XR's most loved consumer products (Supernatural, Symphoni, ShapesXR, and Walkabout Mini Golf) sit down to share the design instincts, engineering tradeoffs, and details behind experiences players return to. Through multi-platform releases, multiplayer rollouts, hardware launches, and platform features, they'll trade hard-won lessons on scope, performance, and standalone hardware constraints. Expect honest reflections on the parts of XR development no tutorial prepares you for: the realities of working across VR, MR, and AR pipelines, how small teams hit big quality bars on standalone hardware, and the shared instincts that keep showing up across very different products.
sessions.next: Android XR and Project Aura: Enterprise XR Gets Real

sessions.title: Android XR and Project Aura: Enterprise XR Gets Real
sessions.speaker: Amir Khorram - XREAL; Ben Stein - Qualcomm; Sean Ginevan - Google
sessions.description: Powered by Snapdragon and built in collaboration with Google, Android XR and XREAL’s Project Aura mark a new reality for enterprise‑ready XR. This session explores how platform and ecosystem advancements are improving device management, security, and AI‑driven capabilities for enterprise use cases. Attendees will learn how organizations and developers can evaluate, build, and scale on Android XR today as part of their enterprise roadmap.
sessions.next: Building Together: Co-Designing the Future with Wearable AI

sessions.title: Building Together: Co-Designing the Future with Wearable AI
sessions.speaker: Jacklyn Doig-Keys - Meta
sessions.description: Wearable AI has the potential to expand accessibility, agency, and autonomy—supporting everything from personal life management and education to workforce development, economic opportunity, and greater independence in daily routines. But realizing that potential depends on who is at the table from the start. In this session, Jacklyn Doig-Keys will share how Meta has partnered with a broad range of user cohorts and community experts to co-design wearable AI products—translating lived experience into better product outcomes. Through real-world examples, she’ll highlight what meaningful collaboration looks like in practice, what we’ve learned, and where we still need community leadership. You’ll also get an overview of how Meta’s developer tools are helping communities build and scale high-impact use cases—and how your organization can engage, influence, and co-create the future of wearable AI.
sessions.next: The UGC Creator Path in XR

sessions.title: The UGC Creator Path in XR
sessions.speaker: Dominick Frederick - Edge Of Creation; Natasha Gubernov; Deepak Nair
sessions.description: User-generated content (UGC) continues to grow in both volume and influence, and the XR industry is no exception. As audiences are increasingly overwhelmed by content from every direction, attention naturally shifts toward shorter-form gaming and more immediate experiences. At the same time, social disconnection is at an all-time high, driving people to seek out spaces that feel authentic, participatory, and community-driven. XR user-generated content enables creators to build immersive worlds shaped by play, experimentation, and collaboration. This talk explores the UGC creator path in XR: from player to creator to full-stack developer, and how accessible tools, playful iteration, and community feedback allow meaningful experiences to emerge without traditional pipelines or large teams. Drawing from the real-world experience of multi-award-winning creators, this session focuses on the practical realities of building as a small team or solo creator. Speakers will share concrete approaches to developing narrative, defining a clear artistic vision, and designing for player experience, while demonstrating how strong creative direction, intentional constraints, and continuous experimentation can often matter more than deep technical specialization. Attendees will leave with practical insights on how to turn personal ideas into achievable goals, balance artistic expression with interactivity, and grow XR experiences with their community.
sessions.next: Roundtable Discussions: Healthcare - Scaling and Solutions

sessions.title: Roundtable Discussions: Healthcare - Scaling and Solutions
sessions.speaker: José Ferrer Costa - Badalona Serveis Assistencials | Open University of Catalonia; Alley Till - Acceso Salud Virtual; Robin White Owen - MediaCombo/ViTaLS
sessions.description: Table #1: Getting Beyond the Pilot to Make XR Work in Real Healthcare Leader: José FERRER COSTA, Research & Innovation Many XR healthcare pilots show promise but fail to survive routine clinical practice. This roundtable focuses on what actually allows immersive tools to be used by real professionals with real patients: workflow fit, responsibility, safety and long-term adoption. Join clinicians and developers discussing how XR moves from impressive demo to everyday care. Table #2: Global Telehealth: Solutions, Gaps & Partnerships Alley Till, Bates Technical College Roundtable on interoperable platforms integrating EHRs, wearables, and spatial AI. Participants assess solutions, identify barriers, and form partnerships to scale virtual care internationally. Table #3: Transforming Health Professions Education with XR: Aligning Leadership Around the Mission Robin White Owen, MediaCombo Roundtable on defining strategies and optimal features, and locating resources required to move from successful pilots to longitudinal programs. Participants will bring their experiences, problems and questions to our discussion.
sessions.next: Building XR Digital Twins for Selection, Training, and Performance Monitoring

sessions.title: Building XR Digital Twins for Selection, Training, and Performance Monitoring
sessions.speaker: Dr. Gergely Kovacs - VIKI - Védelmi Innovációs Kutatóintézet - Defence Innovation Institute XR Centre of Excellence
sessions.description: XR is often used for training, but I use it to understand how people perform under pressure. In this talk, I present a practical XR digital twin approach that I use for selection, training, and continuous performance monitoring. Through VR scenarios, I observe physical and cognitive performance, decision-making, and stress response in a controlled and repeatable way, supported by physiological and behavioral data. The system combines VR-based assessment with EEG, EMG, and HRV measurements, along with in-headset telemetry and biometric data. These signals make it possible to go beyond subjective evaluation and build functional performance personas based on how individuals actually react, adapt, and recover under load. The approach is based on real-world use and a 548-participant study, linking VR assessment directly to physical and cognitive development. I also show how trainers can quickly build and adapt VR scenarios using simple content tools, without technical complexity. The focus is practical: what works in everyday use, what scales, and how XR digital twins can move beyond experimentation into reliable performance systems.
sessions.next: Creating a Website Environment for Apple Vision Pro

sessions.title: Creating a Website Environment for Apple Vision Pro
sessions.speaker: Steve Talkowski - Sketchbot Studios
sessions.description: Steve Talkowski breaks down the process for creating a 3d website environment - with animation and audio - viewable via Safari on Apple Vision Pro.
sessions.next: XR Sports Alliance x FISE: First Learnings from Immersive Sports at a Live Action Event

sessions.title: XR Sports Alliance x FISE: First Learnings from Immersive Sports at a Live Action Event
sessions.speaker: Lucy Trang Nguyen - Accedo; Kendall Goodden - FISE World Series
sessions.description: What does it actually take to deploy immersive sports content at a live urban sports festival? In this session, Kendall Goodden, Senior Global Partnerships Manager at FISE, and Lucy Trang Nguyen, Director of Strategic Alliances & Business Development, Emerging Technologies at Accedo, share first-hand learnings from their joint experiment at FISE Montpellier 2026. The project brought fans inside the live experience through an immersive editorial vlog — combining stereoscopic 180° video, drone footage, and 2D content — built for Meta Quest and Android XR XREAL Aura glasses. Conceived as structured experimentation, it was designed to generate real data across nine objectives covering fan-perceived quality on MR headsets vs. AR glasses, hybrid content formats, production scalability, and immersive sponsorship value, and more. The session covers what the experiment set out to learn, how it was structured to generate meaningful data, and what the early results reveal — offering evidence-grounded takeaways on what works, what doesn't, and what the industry still needs to solve.
sessions.next: Unlocking Presence: How AI Glasses are Changing the Way We Live

sessions.title: Unlocking Presence: How AI Glasses are Changing the Way We Live
sessions.speaker: Anna Gordon - Meta; Kevin Lang - Agerpoint; Quinn Taber - Immerse; Thai Nguyen - Diverge, a Hensel Phelps Company
sessions.description: AI glasses are fundamentally changing how we relate to technology and the world around us. At Meta, we've been on a journey to deliver on this promise at scale — recognizing that as AI better understands our physical environment, it can help us deepen our connection to the world and people around us. Today, with growing global adoption of our AI glasses, that vision is becoming reality. In this keynote, we'll explore how the technology is evolving from reactive tools into proactive, context-aware companions, followed by a panel of innovators who will discuss how AI glasses are already reshaping daily life — and how they see this evolution unfolding in the coming years.
sessions.next: The New Cinema: Designing & Monetizing Shared, Transformative Immersive Experiences

sessions.title: The New Cinema: Designing & Monetizing Shared, Transformative Immersive Experiences
sessions.speaker: Rob Shalhoub - Fever; Michaela Ternasky-Holland - MTH Studio; Neville Spiteri - Wevr; Amy Jupiter - ByJove, LLC
sessions.description: As location-based entertainment evolves from experimental novelty into essential cultural destinations, a new form of cinema is emerging that transforms passive viewers into active participants who experience stories together with friends and family. This panel brings together pioneering creators who are redefining immersive entertainment through powerful spatial storytelling and meaningful shared presence. From breathtaking ocean expeditions to reimagined literary classics, these experiences allow groups to explore, interact, and create memories side-by-side in virtual worlds that feel vividly real. And unlike even the most elaborate theme park, XR can transport us directly into the worlds of our most beloved stories—not as observers, but as participants in the narrative itself, exploring fantastical environments and iconic moments that would be impossible to build in physical space. The discussion will explore the creative, technical, and business challenges of building the “New Cinema,” including: Designing emotionally resonant, room-scale XR experiences that foster genuine interaction and shared presence between participants Creating social dynamics where friends and family can explore, play, and connect with each other inside the story, leaving transformed Developing sustainable business models for location-based immersive experiences that require positive unit economics with global partnerships Whether you’re developing your first immersive project or your fiftieth, this session delivers practical, battle-tested insights from creators who have successfully turned transformative, socially engaging ideas into commercially viable experiences.

Presenters will be available to discuss their work and provide demos during this hour. (Posters will remain available to view throughout all AWE USA 2026 Expo Hall hours.)

sessions.next: Feature Film-Quality 6DoF: Exploring the Future of Cinematic Media

sessions.title: Feature Film-Quality 6DoF: Exploring the Future of Cinematic Media
sessions.speaker: Greg Downing - Hyperacuity
sessions.description: This session explores how feature-grade 6DoF can help define the future of cinematic media. Through two narrative pieces produced using V-Nova’s PresentZ technology — one animated, and one live-action short captured with a splat-like workflow enhanced by visual effects — the talk examines how high-end animation, VFX, and cinematic production tools can be adapted for immersive storytelling. Using open-source films as a creative testbed, these projects explore how familiar cinematic language can evolve into spatial experiences that preserve image quality, authorial intent, and audience comfort. The result points toward a new kind of media: immersive, narrative, and built for presence and comfort.
sessions.next: PANEL: Deploying XR Devices with Patients

sessions.title: PANEL: Deploying XR Devices with Patients
sessions.speaker: Omead Sinai - Rock Paper Reality; Haipeng (Mark) Zhang - American Medical Extended Reality Association; Christine Ladd - Samsung; Miguel Carrazza Morales - Abbott Laboratories
sessions.description: XR has the potential to fundamentally transform the patient experience in healthcare by reducing anxiety, managing pain, or making difficult procedures more tolerable. This panel brings together healthcare providers, physicians, hardware manufacturers, and developers who are pioneering patient-facing XR deployments during blood draws, procedures, and treatment. We'll share what we've learned putting headsets on real patients alongside honest challenges from onboarding to technical hurdles. Expect practical insights on what works and what doesn't, and why this has the potential to improve the patient experience in healthcare.
sessions.next: Spatial AI: Transforming the Workspace

sessions.title: Spatial AI: Transforming the Workspace
sessions.speaker: Matt Fedorovich - Insight Enterprises
sessions.description: Spatial Computing provides the canvas, but AI provides the engine. As industries scale their XR deployments, Spatial AI is emerging as the critical bridge between digital data and physical operations. This session breaks down the real-world applications of Spatial AI in the enterprise today. We will explore how AI-driven spatial mapping, intelligent digital twins, and real-time contextual assistance are revolutionizing training, manufacturing, and remote collaboration. Join us to cut through the hype and learn actionable strategies for integrating Spatial AI into your business operations to drive measurable ROI.

sessions.title: Leveraging Immersive XR Technology in Manufacturing at General Motors
sessions.speaker: Dean Hu - General Motors; Michael Williams - General Motors; Jason Kohnert - General Motors
sessions.description: XR is reshaping how vehicles are designed and built. In this session, GM virtual manufacturing engineers discuss how immersive technologies are transforming the manufacturing lifecycle; from virtual design and validation to operator training and digital twin-enabled production operations. sessions.next: The Future of Ecommerce Is Spatial Commerce

sessions.title: The Future of Ecommerce Is Spatial Commerce
sessions.speaker: Alex Otwell - Vizbl Inc.
sessions.description: For more than a decade, augmented reality promised to transform ecommerce, retail, and customer engagement. But limited mobile hardware, fragmented ecosystems, and inconsistent user experiences kept AR trapped in isolated demos, standalone applications, and experimental experiences rather than commercially scalable products. That is now changing. In this session, Vizbl explores the technological shift enabling AR to become commercially viable at scale. The session examines why early AR struggled to build consumer trust, how modern AR is improving buying confidence and conversion rates, and why infrastructure—not visualization—is the true key to scalable adoption. The presentation covers the evolution of mobile hardware, AI-assisted 3D workflows, cloud rendering systems, WebAR stability, ecommerce integrations, and enterprise deployment strategies that are reshaping the future of spatial computing. Attendees will learn how spatial experiences are beginning to influence purchasing behavior and why infrastructure is becoming the defining layer of next-generation ecommerce. Learn more about Vizbl’s end-to-end AR platform for content generation, optimization, cloud delivery, enterprise deployment, and developer integration. Because AR is no longer just visualization. AR is becoming infrastructure.
sessions.next: PANEL: glTF / USD Interoperability Workflows

sessions.title: PANEL: glTF / USD Interoperability Workflows
sessions.speaker: Ashley Crowder - VNTANA; Alexey Medvedev - Meta; Henrik Edstrom - Cesium; Nick Porcino - Pixar
sessions.description: This panel examines practical interoperability workflows between glTF and USD, highlighting recent tooling and pipeline updates that address long-standing community pain points. Panelists will discuss remaining gaps and mitigation strategies, including best practices, tooling, and extension recommendations. Special attention will be given to material system translation, shading fidelity, and approaches for interoperating advanced representations such as Gaussian splats. Attendees will gain actionable guidance for building resilient, cross-tool workflows that preserve intent, performance, and visual quality across platforms.
sessions.next: PANEL: Agentic AI as a Tool for Enterprise Growth

sessions.title: PANEL: Agentic AI as a Tool for Enterprise Growth
sessions.speaker: Stephanie Montgomery - XR Association; Andy O'Sullivan - Liberty Mutual; Susan Persky - National Institutes of Health
sessions.description: Enterprise speakers will share an overview of how agentic AI systems support enterprise growth. sessions.next: RAID: Three Use Cases for "Real-Time Animated Interactive Drama"

sessions.title: RAID: Three Use Cases for "Real-Time Animated Interactive Drama"
sessions.speaker: Pascal Langdale - Collective Intent
sessions.description: I presented a Realtime Animated Interactive Drama at AWE in 2015. The concept worked. The technology wasn't ready. A decade later, its time has come. RAID combines motion capture, game engines, and live audience interactivity, to be performed simultaneously across the performance venue, VR, and stream. The audience doesn't just watch. They change what happens. In this talk, I'll break down what shifted in the last ten years, why AI is making live performance more valuable (not less), and three practical use cases: whether you're in games, experiential marketing, interactive storytelling, or live entertainment. I'll also share what we learned building the RAID - "Compound Illusion" with Game On Montreal - what worked, what broke, and what surprised us.

sessions.speaker: Alvin Graylin - Our Next Reality | Virtual World Society; Leslie Shannon - Virtual World Society; Avi Bar-Zeev - XR Guild; Laura Kusumoto - IterationZero; Tom Furness - Virtual World Society - Light School
sessions.description: The nonprofit Virtual World Society (VWS) invites you to join us for a two-pronged meetup that will look back at XR's past while inviting your thoughts about XR's future. VWS is hosting a fundraising auction to support its mission of shaping a better future with humanity-focused emerging technologies, and at this meetup you'll be able to see most of the auction items first-hand before placing final bids. (The actual auction will conclude at the end of AWE on Thursday.) After a review of the key auction items, VWS leaders including Tom Furness, Alvin Graylin, Laura Kusumoto, Avi Bar-Zeev, and Leslie Shannon will host a freewheeling brainstorm with the group focused on the future of XR, and where the VWS can best contribute to it. Where should XR go from here? What are the main challenges that still need to be addressed? How can we at VWS and all of us make sure that humanity is always in the forefront of this technology? After two days of AWE experiences, you'll have opinions, and this is the forum to bring and share them. We can't wait to hear what you think!
