sessions.next: Beyond XR: The Shift Toward Spatial, Immersive Digital Experiences

sessions.title: Beyond XR: The Shift Toward Spatial, Immersive Digital Experiences
sessions.speaker: Jochem Taminiau - Leia Inc.
sessions.description: The industry talks about AR, VR, MR, and XR — but the real shift isn’t about labels. It’s about how digital content is evolving into immersive experiences. This session explores how spatial interaction is emerging as a new foundation for digital experiences — delivered directly through displays on the devices people already use, from phones and tablets to laptops and monitors. By making content immersive on everyday screens, teams can better understand information, collaborate more effectively, and move faster across workflows. Jochem Taminiau will share how these capabilities are already being applied across industries, and why immersive, display-based experiences are becoming more accessible, practical, and scalable — without the need for headsets.
sessions.next: The Airport Decision Engine: Real-Time Digital Twins for Complex Operations

sessions.title: The Airport Decision Engine: Real-Time Digital Twins for Complex Operations
sessions.speaker: Kelly Watt - Digital Twin Consulting
sessions.description: Airports are among the most complex, dynamic environments in the world - where passenger flow, aircraft operations, security, and commercial performance must operate in sync. While digital twins have been widely discussed, the industry is now moving beyond static visualization toward real-time, operational digital twins that act as decision engines. This session explores how next-generation airport digital twins- integrating IoT, LiDAR, video analytics, GIS, BIM, Business Systems and AI - are evolving into immersive, spatial computing platforms that enable real-time situational awareness, predictive analytics, and operational optimization. Drawing from live deployments and case studies at major international airports, including enterprise-scale programs at Dallas Fort Worth International Airport, this presentation introduces the concept of the “motional digital twin” - a system that models and responds to movement across passengers, vehicles, and assets in real time. Attendees will learn how these systems: Improve passenger flow and reduce congestion Enable predictive operations and simulation-driven planning Unlock non-aeronautical revenue through spatial intelligence Bridge digital twins with XR interfaces for operational teams This talk connects the dots between digital twins and XR, showing how immersive interfaces are becoming the next layer of interaction for complex infrastructure systems.
sessions.next: Explore the Experiences of Creators Showing Their XR Work at Festivals

sessions.title: Explore the Experiences of Creators Showing Their XR Work at Festivals
sessions.speaker: Jacquelyn Morie - All These Worlds, LLC; Lori H. Schwartz - StoryTech
sessions.description: Come share your experience of getting work into festivals large and small. This will be a drop-in, join us at the table, for some story swapping about these important events.
sessions.next: Optimizing XR Performance - Navigating the Texture Compression Landscape

sessions.title: Optimizing XR Performance - Navigating the Texture Compression Landscape
sessions.speaker: Leonard Daly - Daly Realism; Eric Griffith - Meta; Stephanie Hurlburt - Binomial; Amra Tareen - ALL3D
sessions.description: Texture bandwidth and memory remain critical constraints for XR experiences across web, mobile, and native platforms. At the same time, creators must navigate a fragmented texture compression landscape spanning GPU- and platform-specific formats such as ASTC, BCn/DXTn, and ETC2 as well as emerging neural compression techniques. This panel will explore how teams can navigate compression tradeoffs while maintaining performance, visual quality, and portability. We’ll discuss compression decision points along the modern XR pipeline across engines and runtimes, and explore how to effectively manage multiple compression targets. We’ll also take a look at how container formats such as KTX 2.0 fit into the modern content pipeline enabling scalable delivery of multiple compression formats and allowing teams to deploy high-performance, portable textures even as new formats and hardware capabilities emerge.
sessions.next: New Agentic 3D Workflows at Worlds in Action Hack LA

sessions.speaker: Ferhan Ozkan - XR Bootcamp
sessions.description: Right before #SIGGRAPH2026, founders, researchers, engineers, VFX artists, game developers, and spatial computing pioneers will come together in Los Angeles to prototype the next generation of AI-native 3D experiences. ​If you’re building the future of: ​AI-generated 3D worlds ​Spatial interfaces ​Interactive agents ​XR workflows ​Simulation systems ​Generative environments ​Human-AI interaction in 3D space ​…this is your Hack! Join Ferhan Ozkan, co-founder of XR Bootcamp, for an overview of Worlds in Action Hack. Click here to register on Luma.
sessions.next: Where XR Meets Real Science: Unlocking Insights in Disaster, Space Weather & Earth Observation

sessions.title: Where XR Meets Real Science: Unlocking Insights in Disaster, Space Weather & Earth Observation
sessions.speaker: Disha Sardana - NASA
sessions.description: The way we interact with data is constantly evolving. As scientific datasets grow in complexity -- spanning real-time disaster response, dynamic space weather events, and large-scale Earth observation -- traditional visualization tools often fall short. This talk makes the case that mixed reality isn't just a better screen: it's a fundamentally different way to think with data. Drawing on three real-world case studies built in mixed reality environments, I present research-backed evidence that immersive, multimodal environments measurably improve scientific pattern recognition and analytical confidence. In a user study with 50 participants analyzing space weather datasets, audio-visual MR conditions produced 70% more identified trends than visual-only analysis. A parallel system for earthquake disaster response demonstrates how embodied interaction with spatiotemporal data enables faster, more intuitive situational awareness. A third case study extends these principles to Earth observation, exploring how MR can make NASA's complex geophysical datasets more accessible and actionable for researchers and decision-makers. Across all three domains, the same design principles emerge: spatial audio, coordinated multiple views, and human-centered interaction drive real analytical gains. Attendees will leave with concrete, research-backed takeaways on what works, what doesn't, and where immersive analytics is headed next.
sessions.next: PANEL: Beyond Impressions: Measuring Phygital Fashion and XR Brand Play

sessions.title: PANEL: Beyond Impressions: Measuring Phygital Fashion and XR Brand Play
sessions.speaker: Ian Harrison - SNEAKAR.IO; Dreka Gates - DrekAI; Emery Morrison - FR8QU8NCY; Charlene Nichols - The Omniverse City
sessions.description: Brands don’t just need impressions; they need phygital systems that turn culture into measurable attention. In this session, we’ll show how SNEAKAR XR, DREKA AI, Fr8qu8ncy, and The Omniverse City are bridging immersive gamification and real‑world XR activations into one continuous, trackable brand loop. We’ll tap into the “ENVY fashion tycoon” mindset: fashion as a game, a status layer, and a utility layer for communities, not just a look. Together we’ll break down how digital couture, avatar skins, and in‑world gear become levers for fandom, retention, and revenue inside immersive environments and creator‑driven social worlds. Using live examples, we’ll explore how branded skins, digital merch, and functional gameplay items turn CPG and fashion brands into playable media—with WebXR and IRL activations acting as the on‑ramp. That includes redemption mechanics that connect a skin earned in‑game to a product in hand, closing the loop between the digital and the physical. You’ll leave with a concrete blueprint showing: how to design fashion‑forward XR experiences what to measure (sessions, dwell, redemptions, repeat play) how to package those metrics so brands and agencies can treat immersive campaigns like any other media buy—only with deeper participation and higher‑value engagement.
sessions.next: The Future of Sports Fan Engagement

sessions.title: The Future of Sports Fan Engagement
sessions.speaker: Patrick Costello - Qualcomm; Matt Coleman - FansXR; Emma Wakely - Snap; Essem Harris - National Basketball Association (NBA)
sessions.description: Explore how XR, AI, smart glasses, mobile technology, prediction markets, and immersive fan experiences are transforming the future of sports entertainment. From the NFL to next-generation interactive broadcasts, this session examines how leagues, brands, and technology platforms can engage the connected superfan of 2030 and beyond.
sessions.next: The Augmented Imagination (AI 2.0)

sessions.title: The Augmented Imagination (AI 2.0)
sessions.speaker: Christopher Stapleton - SIMIOSYS, LLC
sessions.description: Augment the Imagination; Empower Humanity Imagination has no boundaries—unless we impose them. To realize the full potential of Spatial Artificial Intelligence, we must focus not just on technological outputs but on human outcomes. Technology doesn’t care about humanity; people do. When imagination meets Spatial AI, innovation gains purpose—empowering individuals to envision who they might become and the world they can help create. In this session, Christopher Stapleton explores The Conversational Embodied Story—a framework that connects people and place through mixed realities. Drawing on decades of cross‑sector work—from theme parks to rehabilitation clinics, training environments to civic experiences—Stapleton shows how Spatial AI 2.0 can transform imagination into action and strategy into human-centered innovation. His “Roadmapping Innovation” approach and “Mixing Realities” method integrate engagement strategies with story, play, and games as practical tools for design and collaboration. This has led to his winning of the 2025 IEEE “Test of Time Award” for pioneering work in Mixed Reality. Stapleton has proven ways to turn emerging technologies into meaningful, measurable, and transformative experiences. He applied this to global entertainment and technology brands such as Universal, Disney, Canon, Technicolor, NASA and the National Science Foundation.
sessions.next: Making Magical Mixed Reality Games

sessions.title: Making Magical Mixed Reality Games
sessions.speaker: Nicole Lazzaro - XEODesign Inc
sessions.description: In a world where anything is possible, how do you create a sense of mystery and magic for the player? How do you create game mechanics that blend and leverage the player’s world which could be anywhere? Together let's dive into XR design advancements of leading games on the Apple Vision Pro, Meta Quest, Tilt Five, such as Starship Home, Shattered, as well as our own game Follow the White Rabbit. This lively presentation unpacks the opportunities and challenges for immersive gameplay as well as how to elicit the right player emotions for your idea. From augmented reality to epic virtual reality adventures hop on board as we discuss the design, technology, and player experience challenges reshaping mixed reality gaming. Join us for a captivating journey into the bright future of mixed reality games where imagination meets technology to redefine the gaming experience.
sessions.next: Environment Modeling of Customer Spaces with Southwest Airlines

sessions.title: Environment Modeling of Customer Spaces with Southwest Airlines
sessions.speaker: Jeffrey Berthiaume - Southwest Airlines; Justin Leger - Southwest Airlines
sessions.description: How does a major airline prototype the future of customer and employee spaces? Join the Southwest Airlines Technology Innovation team for a deep dive into their transition from physical foam core modeling to MockThru, a custom-built spatial application. This session showcases the journey of visualizing diverse environments through immersive walkthroughs, moving beyond traditional mockups into high-fidelity digital spaces. We’ll discuss the strategic decision to develop a custom software solution, why the Apple Vision Pro was the right hardware choice for this use case, and what the team is exploring next as they expand the boundaries of spatial environment modeling.
sessions.next: 17th Annual Auggie Awards and Hall of Fame Ceremony

sessions.title: 17th Annual Auggie Awards and Hall of Fame Ceremony
sessions.speaker: Ori Inbar - AWE & UnitedXR
sessions.description: The annual Auggie Awards have been the most recognized industry AR & VR awards show in the world since 2010. The Auggies continue to showcase the best of the best in augmented and virtual reality and award winners will be presented with a prestigious Auggie Award live on stage. We will also welcome new inductees to the Hall of Fame, which honors visionaries who have made this industry possible. Join us!
sessions.next: The Wild West of Storytelling: From Hollywood Legacy to Immersive Worlds

sessions.title: The Wild West of Storytelling: From Hollywood Legacy to Immersive Worlds
sessions.speaker: Travis Cloyd - California Institute of the Arts; Deborah Worrell - Observe Media; Billy Zane - The Convergence Lab
sessions.description: The future of storytelling is no longer confined to the screen—it’s unfolding across physical, digital, and spatial worlds in real time. It’s the wild-west all over again. This panel brings together Billy Zane, an iconic Hollywood storyteller whose career spans legendary films like Back to the Future, Titanic, Zoolander, The Phantom, Dead Calm, and many more—and Travis Cloyd, Director of the D.R.E.A.M.S. program and professor at California Institute of the Arts, who is actively building the bridge between enterprise technology, immersive media, and next generation education. Moderated by Deborah Worrell, a seasoned entertainment technology executive, this conversation explores how storytelling is evolving in an era defined by AI, XR, and Location-Based Entertainment (LBE)—and what it truly means to create experiences that go beyond the rectangular screen.
sessions.next: Building a 4D God's Eye View: The Intelligence Monopoly Is Over

sessions.title: Building a 4D God's Eye View: The Intelligence Monopoly Is Over
sessions.speaker: Bilawal Sidhu - TED Conferences
sessions.description: I sat on my couch one Saturday and reconstructed a war. When the Iran strikes started, I told my army of AI agents to start banking every public data feed it could find before the caches cleared. By Sunday I had a 4D replay of Operation Epic Fury on a 3D globe -- commercial flights rerouting, spy satellites passing overhead, GPS jamming intensifying, and ships fleeing the Strait of Hormuz. Observers called it "vibe coding Palantir." That was a weekend project, and yet this is just the beginning. I'll show you what's possible now, what's coming, and what happens when Earth itself becomes a read/write database for the world's open source intelligence.
sessions.next: PANEL: Demystifying World Models

sessions.title: PANEL: Demystifying World Models
sessions.speaker: Bilawal Sidhu - TED Conferences; Matt Miesnieks - Stealth Mode AI Startup; Sharon Lee - Moonlake AI
sessions.description: World models may be the most important -- and least agreed-upon -- idea in AI right now. We're putting builders with very different bets on one stage to argue what a world model even is, whose approach pays off, and what changes when machines can simulate reality.
sessions.next: Engineering Empathy: Biosensing, Emotion, and the Next Layer of XR

sessions.title: Engineering Empathy: Biosensing, Emotion, and the Next Layer of XR
sessions.speaker: Conor Russomanno - OpenBCI, Inc.
sessions.description: Extended reality has achieved immersion and interaction, but it has not yet grasped understanding. The next evolution of XR involves systems that can sense and respond to human emotions. As biosensing becomes part of the XR technology toolkit, we can design experiences that recognize physiological and emotional contexts. This is not for manipulation, but to strengthen connections. This session looks at how multimodal biometrics, including EEG, EDA, HRV, EMG, and eye tracking, can inform real-time emotional modeling and adaptive interfaces. We will introduce the idea of The Empathy Stack, a new design layer that connects biosensing with emotion-aware interaction. We’ll discuss how it can promote empathy among users and within the teams that create XR systems. Attendees will learn practical strategies for ethically integrating biosensing data, creating emotionally intelligent environments, and shaping the next generation of human-centered XR.
sessions.next: From Operating Room to AR Reading Room: Building Trust Through XR

sessions.title: From Operating Room to AR Reading Room: Building Trust Through XR
sessions.speaker: Steven Christian - Iltopia; Patrick Thomas MD, MMCI, FACS - University of Nebraska Medical Center
sessions.description: Trust is vital in healthcare, yet underserved communities often lack culturally responsive tools. Dr. Patrick Thomas and Steven Christian show how XR—from surgical walkthroughs to AR comics—bridges the trust gap by making medical information clear, relatable, and empowering. Real-world case studies demonstrate impact across hospital and community settings.

sessions.title: Introducing the New TactGlove DK3: How We Engineered Whole-Hand Haptics
sessions.speaker: Kiuk Gwak - bHaptics
sessions.description: As leading XR headsets embrace hand-native input and physical AI drives surging demand for dexterous robot control, the need for haptic gloves that deliver rich, nuanced feedback across the entire hand has never been greater. The TactGlove DK3, unveiled by bHaptics at AWE 2026, takes a significant step forward from its predecessor. Two newly added palm motors extend the haptic canvas across the full hand, while a high-fidelity wrist-mounted VCM delivers the sharp, realistic impacts needed for truly convincing interaction. In this session, Dr. Gwak will walk through the engineering and algorithms behind the TactGlove DK3 and demonstrate how it integrates with bHaptics' authoring software and SDKs to enable immersive haptic experiences across XR and beyond.
sessions.next: PANEL: BurnerSphere: Scaling the Spirit of Black Rock City into a Persistent Social Metaverse

sessions.title: PANEL: BurnerSphere: Scaling the Spirit of Black Rock City into a Persistent Social Metaverse
sessions.speaker: Athena Demos - Big Rock Creative & BurnerSphere; Justin Gunn - BurnerSphere; Doug Jacobson - Big Rock Creative & BurnerSphere; Gregory Panos - Persona Foundation
sessions.description: Dive into the digital dust of BurnerSphere, an artistic spatially navigable social XR world that redefines the boundaries of immersive storytelling. Born from the award-winning BRCvr and developed in collaboration with the Burning Man Project, this platform is far more than a digital twin—it is a living, persistent ecosystem designed to capture and preserve the ephemeral culture of Black Rock City. Moderated by XR Hall-of-Fame Award recipient Gregory Panos, this panel features Athena Demos, Doug Jacobson, and Justin Gunn to explore the project’s journey from its pandemic-era origins to its current state as a global community hub. The discussion will bridge the gap between abstract philosophy and the "present-day challenges" of implementation, specifically focusing on the friction of merging high-fidelity 360°/180° media with real-time social agency. Key Discussion Points: The Philosophy of Participation: Shifting the user mindset from "Guest" to "Camp Mate" to enforce Radical Inclusion and Communal Effort. Implementation Hurdles: Navigating the technical complexities of maintaining social interactivity within high-resolution, spatially navigated environments. Spatial Inclusion: Exploring how BurnerSphere utilizes spatial computing to foster genuine human connection and provide accessibility to those unable to attend the physical event. Attendees will gain actionable insights on building large-scale social VR environments that prioritize human connection over passive consumption. This session serves as a definitive case study in spatial Inclusion, proving that XR can successfully scale human culture and inspire year-round creativity across the globe.

sessions.next: From XR Vision to the Shop Floor: A Heavy Industry Case Study

sessions.title: From XR Vision to the Shop Floor: A Heavy Industry Case Study
sessions.speaker: Dan Cui - Shiloh Strategic Marketing; Randolph Warnaar - Fabstation; Alida Rehm - Schuff Steel
sessions.description: This session examines how XR transitioned from concept to operational tool inside a real structural steel fabrication environment. Through Schuff Steel's journey, we explore how a heavy industrial organization was introduced to XR, the production challenges it faced, and why the high cost of fabrication mistakes made change necessary. In steel fabrication, errors are expensive. A misread drawing, incorrect weld, or dimensional inaccuracy can trigger material waste, rework, schedule delays, and margin erosion. As complexity increased and workforce pressures grew, Schuff Steel evaluated whether immersive visualization tools could improve precision, strengthen engineering-to-shop communication, and reduce costly mistakes. XR was not introduced as innovation theater, but as a productivity tool. Success required workforce trust, operational ownership, and alignment with existing fabrication workflows. This session discusses how acceptance was achieved in a blue-collar production environment, how XR moved from pilot to embedded process, and how impact was measured through reduced rework, improved validation, and greater production confidence. Grounded in real-world implementation, this case study connects industry vision with measurable shop-floor results. Discussion Themes The true cost of error in steel fabrication Introducing XR in a highly industrial workforce From pilot to process integration Measuring ROI: error reduction, efficiency, and margin protection Strategic guidance for heavy industry leaders Key Takeaways A realistic view of XR adoption in heavy industry Insight into how operational leaders evaluate immersive tools Practical lessons for integrating XR into precision-driven environments
sessions.next: Building Smart Glasses People Actually Wear: A Validation Framework for Product Leaders

sessions.title: Building Smart Glasses People Actually Wear: A Validation Framework for Product Leaders
sessions.speaker: Tinku Malayil Jose - Quest Global; Thomas Winkler - Quest Global
sessions.description: Smart glasses do not fail because of missing features. They fail because the gap between demo magic and daily wearability is rarely validated early, systematically, and cross-functionally. System validation is often treated as a late-stage technical gate, a checklist to confirm compliance with specifications. In reality, it is one of the most powerful strategic tools available to product and engineering leaders building AI wearables. In this session, Thomas Winkler and Tinku Jose from Quest Global present a practical validation framework that bridges desirability and feasibility across the entire development lifecycle, starting from the first prototype. Drawing from real-world programs across smart devices and AI-enabled hardware, they show how to translate user research, market insights, and brand ambition into measurable KPIs and validation plans. Expect real-world examples and best-practice lessons to shift teams from component testing to experience-level validation - so your device ships not just as an engineering feat, but as something people choose to wear every day.
sessions.next: Building the Infrastructure Behind Live Spatial Experiences

sessions.title: Building the Infrastructure Behind Live Spatial Experiences
sessions.speaker: Daniel Zeitman - Spatial Explorer Labs
sessions.description: Spatial computing is moving beyond static environments and passive immersive video. The next phase is live, adaptive, and state-driven. This talk explores the infrastructure required to support real-time spatial experiences — systems that combine live capture, dynamic scene representation, synchronized inputs, and spatial audio into a cohesive, interactive presence. Rather than focusing on content creation or device capabilities, this session examines the underlying architecture: real-time reconstruction splat-based rendering approaches pose and sync management bandwidth optimization AI-powered spatial audio workflows The central argument is simple: scalable spatial experiences depend on infrastructure that can manage and adapt complex spatial states in real time. Without that layer, immersive systems remain limited to static or pre-produced content. Attendees will leave with a systems-level understanding of what must exist beneath live spatial experiences — and where the next platform opportunities are emerging.

sessions.title: Beyond WOW! Designing Quality VR Experiences for Kids
sessions.speaker: Elizabeth Rood - The Joan Ganz Cooney Center at Sesame Workshop
sessions.description: What is the key to finding success with younger users? Designing responsibly for children requires more than simplifying content. It demands thoughtful alignment with young people's developmental capacities, emotional needs, and family expectations. VR platforms are increasingly full of young people, even though the experiences were not created with young people's unique developmental needs in mind. Many developers lack opportunities to hear directly from young users and their families and may not have reliable information about what kids need to thrive in digital spaces. In this lightning talk, Dr. Elizabeth Rood will share practical takeaways from a recently published set of guides for developers, platform teams, and funders interested in engaging younger audiences in virtual reality experiences. Based on research with pre-teens and their parents, the guides offer actionable insights for creating VR experiences that center emotions, relationships, and autonomy as three north stars of design for young people. The talk will include strategies for developing content that meets kids’ desires while also earning parents' mark of approval as beneficial for development. Walk away inspired with actionable design guidance for creating quality immersive experiences and content for kids, ten and older.

sessions.next: Knowledge Management, XR, Cognitive Science, Computer Graphics History

sessions.title: Knowledge Management, XR, Cognitive Science, Computer Graphics History
sessions.speaker: Dr. Julian Gómez - Computer Graphics History Institute
sessions.description: Knowledge graphs are typically represented as nodes and arcs. A knowledgebase of more than minimal size, however, rapidly leads to the classic hairball problem onscreen. XR is technology that enables drawing a knowledge graph in actual 3-D, and the Venturi science is the needed paradigm shift to interact with a complex display. The basis of Venturi is that the human comes first. In contrast to conventional approaches, which is that there are APIs to technologies and the human maps itself to those APIs, in Venturi there is an API to the human cognitive system, and technologies must interface to that human centered API. In this way, there is not really any user training, as humans learn how to interface to reality from the day they're born. Overall, Venturi is a confluence of IT, KM (semantic web, graph databases, etc.), 3D computer graphics, XR, and built on a base of cognitive science. The Venturi principles are being applied at the Computer Graphics History Institute to build the Cadmus knowledge base. This Institute has a twofold mission: reference and research. First, it will be be a reference site for research and education. It will serve as a research base in several areas, the first being digital knowledge models of history. Another is cognitive based interaction with technology when the interaction focus is completely centered on cognitive abilities. Another area is what kinds of interaction can be built when the basis is cognitive abilities rather than technology features. These missions are designed for mutual community benefit, in both reference and research.

Whether you're building training systems, operational support tools, or customer-facing AI applications, you'll gain actionable insights on bridging the gap between cutting-edge technology and real-world implementation. Key Takeaways: Architectural patterns for integrating LLMs, VR, and Agentic AI in enterprise learning platforms Strategies for designing inclusive, accessible AI systems that work across diverse user populations and operating conditions Frameworks for measuring impact beyond performance metrics—including training effectiveness, learning outcomes, and operational resilience Approaches to AI deployment that prioritize human expertise
sessions.next: Treating Emotional Trauma with XR and AI

sessions.title: Treating Emotional Trauma with XR and AI
sessions.speaker: Albert Skip Rizzo - University of Southern California Institute for Creative Technologies
sessions.description: War is perhaps one of the most challenging situations that a human being can experience. The physical, emotional, cognitive and psychological demands of a combat environment place enormous stress on even the best-prepared military personnel. In 2004, numerous reports suggested that the conflicts in Iraq and Afghanistan were having a significant impact on the mental health of deployed Service Members and Veterans (Hoge et al., 2004). This situation helped motivate efforts to better develop and disseminate evidence-based treatments for PTSD and related psychological health conditions. In response, the USC BRAVEMIND Virtual Reality Exposure Therapy (VRET) system was developed and has since demonstrated positive outcomes in treating combat-related PTSD. BRAVEMIND supports controlled, repeated exposure to trauma-related cues to promote habituation and emotional processing, consistent with extinction-based models of exposure therapy. The talk will address key design, development, and dissemination challenges, highlight lessons learned about reducing barriers to care and supporting efficacy across diverse populations, and close with a brief look at how AI-enabled Virtual Human support agents may extend care beyond the therapy office.

sessions.title: Inventing Global Immersive Public Media
sessions.speaker: Ben Erwin - Academy of Immersive Arts and Sciences
sessions.description: Generations X, Y, and Z grew up on public television and radio. It educated, informed, and expanded cultural awareness through trusted, non-commercial content. The internet has made local and national broadcast markets less relevant. Political forces are defunding public broadcasters. The platforms replacing them prioritize engagement over enrichment. At the same time, immersive media is emerging as a new medium. It places audiences inside stories, to explore environments and ideas. We need to build modern infrastructure to support the creation and distribution of immersive public media that informs, inspires, and serves our interconnected global society. In this talk, Ben Erwin explores a 21st century successor to public media. A global, immersive ecosystem for education, journalism, arts, culture, history, and science, built on open standards, platform-agnostic distribution, and new public-interest funding models.

sessions.next: Toward Evaluating and Securing XR Experiences in Real Time with Large Multimodal Models (LMMs)

sessions.title: Toward Evaluating and Securing XR Experiences in Real Time with Large Multimodal Models (LMMs)
sessions.speaker: Maria Gorlatova - Duke University
sessions.description: When deployed in unconstrained real-world environments, modern mixed reality (MR) experiences can fail in subtle but consequential ways: virtual objects may drift or occlude critical real-world elements; spatial audio cues may contradict visual content; MR-based guidance may suggest actions that are impossible, unsafe, or societally unacceptable. Such highly context-dependent failures, where MR content that is appropriate in one setting becomes unsafe in another, cannot be detected by classical signal processing or conventional ML approaches alone. Recent large multimodal models (LMMs) enable semantic, context-aware monitoring of immersive systems, going far beyond what was previously feasible. By jointly reasoning over video streams captured by MR devices, rendered virtual content, spatial audio, and scene context, these models can identify misaligned overlays, unsafe occlusions, inconsistent audio-visual cues, and policy violations in running MR applications. In this talk, we present an LMM-based MR experience evaluation pipeline that combines foundation-scale multimodal models with auxiliary ML components to analyze continuous multimodal streams and trigger corrective actions when problematic content is detected. A central technical challenge in LMM-based MR evaluation is achieving sufficiently low latency for integration into live MR systems. We describe a system architecture and inference strategy that enables responsive, near–real-time multimodal monitoring while maintaining the breadth of semantic reasoning provided by large models. The methodology presented in this talk received the 2025 IEEE International Symposium on Mixed and Augmented Reality (IEEE ISMAR) Best Paper Award. This research is funded by an NSF AI Institute, a DARPA Young Faculty Award, and a DARPA Director’s Fellowship.
sessions.next: Who Are We Becoming? Why XR, Avatars, and Digital Fashion Are Rewriting Identity

sessions.title: AI-Driven Companion in XR
sessions.speaker: Jason Kao - University of Inland Norway
sessions.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) avatars can simulate human-like interactions, providing personalized experiences in areas such as mental health support and social companionship. The development of AI avatars for companionship has rapidly advanced in recent years, combining natural language processing (NLP), computer vision, and affective computing. These avatars are becoming more capable of engaging in lively, empathetic conversations by recognizing and responding to emotional cues through text and voice. Nowadays, AI avatars primarily facilitate user interaction through screen-based devices such as monitors and smartphones. The visualization of AI avatars depends on the photorealism presented on the display to persuade users of the existence of the companion. Users will not perceive the co-presence, which is the user’s sense of being physically co-located with the companion, due to the lack of the perceptual realism explored specifically in research on extended reality (XR). Accordingly, leveraging visual experience and spatial distance to influence users’ sense of co-presence with an AI companion can facilitate the development of companionship that aims to enhance sensory experience. XR is thus considered technologies enabling the AI avatar to appear in users’ physical environment and interact with physical objects. This session explores the potential of integrating augmented reality (AR) technologies to visualize AI avatars as interactive companions. Using augmented imaging, users can see virtual companions as if they are part of the real environment when viewing through AR glasses. This approach transcends the constraints of screen-based interactions, bringing AI engagement into real-world settings.
sessions.next: Research Posters - Highlighted Projects

sessions.title: Research Posters - Highlighted Projects
sessions.speaker: Michelle Binyan Xu - Northeastern University; Elizabeth Childs - Stanford
sessions.description: The AWE Research Track brings cutting-edge work directly to the expo floor, creating a bridge between research and industry. Throughout the conference, attendees will be able to visit a unique space where researchers, startups, and creators meet to share ideas and push the boundaries of Spatial AI. During this presentation, we'll hear from the presenters of two standout research papers as they share their work on the Builders stage. Please join us as we celebrate progress and excitement in XR.
sessions.next: “It Makes No Sense!” – Perception Across Bodies, Robots, and Virtual Environments

sessions.title: “It Makes No Sense!” – Perception Across Bodies, Robots, and Virtual Environments
sessions.speaker: Jasmine Roberts - Carnegie Mellon Robotics Institute | NASA Marshall Flight Center; Adam Sauer - Circaevum DAO; Dev Kennedy - Motion ML
sessions.description: Robots and virtual environments share a common failure: none can reliably sense the physical world the way biological systems do. In the human body, contact, tension, and slip are processed peripherally before signals ever reach conscious awareness. Achieving this in Physical AI built on inorganic systems is still one of the most formidable open problems facing human-machine interaction. This talk addresses that challenge across three domains: inferring contact from actuation-side signals alone, translating those inferences into feedback in robotic and virtual systems, and reading the human side of the loop through biofeedback. Live demonstrations.
sessions.next: PANEL: How We Can Preserve Online Worlds and Why It Matters

sessions.title: PANEL: How We Can Preserve Online Worlds and Why It Matters
sessions.speaker: Neil Barbour - S&P Global Market Intelligence; Wagner James Au - New World Consulting; Evo Heyning - Realitycraft; Julian Reyes - Virtual Worlds Museum™
sessions.description: Digital environments designed to foster a sense of identity and creativity are core components of the consumer landscape and quickly growing in population. This panel explores what’s driving population growth in virtual worlds and what it will take to further build out sustainable, culturally rich and economically viable ecosystems that can scale. We’ll also examine how AI and spatial computing are reshaping the way worlds are built, experienced and connected. Roblox, Fortnite and Minecraft have become massive population centers in the virtual universe approaching 600 million monthly active users, but this may only be the beginning. The presentation will feature Demonstration of content preservation tools Discussion of the role of AI in virtual world creation and preservation Data-rich illustration of the size, scope and economic opportunity/vulnerability of virtual worlds Examination of immersive and virtual spaces now including thousands of creators and teams building millions of new worlds for social experiences Exploration of the movements in cultural preservation, charting the metaverse, connecting creators and providing easy access to a wide range of virtual immersive learning experiences

sessions.speaker: Mallesham Dasari - Northeastern University; Andrew Martin - Utah State University - Innovation Lab (ILab); Ludovic Attiogbe - Utah State University - Innovation Lab (ILab); Pat Clark - University of Colorado Boulder / Immersive Experience Alliance (IXA); Erin Reilly - University of Texas at Austin; Jonathan David Martin - University of Maryland

sessions.next: XR, AI and the Next Consumer Journey

sessions.title: XR, AI and the Next Consumer Journey
sessions.speaker: Benny Lee - Newell Brands; Louisa Spring - SAM Immersive; David Robustelli - Beyond Studio
sessions.description: As brands compete for attention in an increasingly crowded digital landscape, immersive technologies are redefining how consumers discover, engage with, and connect to products, services, and experiences. From AI-powered virtual humans and spatial commerce to immersive retail and experiential marketing, XR is moving beyond experimentation and becoming a powerful tool for customer engagement and business growth. This panel brings together industry leaders from retail marketing, and brand innovation to explore how XR and AI are reshaping the consumer journey. We'll examine where immersive experiences are delivering measurable value today and what consumers will expect from the next generation of personalized, interactive experiences.
sessions.next: Creating New Formats for Immersive Entertainment

sessions.title: Creating New Formats for Immersive Entertainment
sessions.speaker: Victor Agulhon - Targo
sessions.description: Television has the series. Cinema has the film. Every medium becomes mainstream when it finds the right format. But more than ten years in, immersive entertainment is still searching for the one that will define it. In this talk, Victor Agulhon, Emmy-nominated producer and co-founder of TARGO, explores that search through the making of Crafting Crimes , an interactive video-podcast for Meta Quest. At the crossroads of documentary, podcasting, gaming, and spatial storytelling, Crafting Crimes pioneers a format designed natively for immersive platforms—made possible by new advances in technology, interaction, and distribution. Victor will share the creative philosophy behind the project, the best practices that emerged during production, and the lessons learned while trying to shape what immersive entertainment could become next.
sessions.next: Using Mixed Reality to Reduce Anxiety and Inspire the Next Generation of Blood Donors

sessions.title: Using Mixed Reality to Reduce Anxiety and Inspire the Next Generation of Blood Donors
sessions.speaker: Miguel Carrazza Morales - Abbott Laboratories
sessions.description: Every two seconds, someone in the U.S. needs blood—yet anxiety around donation continues to deter many potential donors, especially younger generations. This session explores how mixed reality can transform the donation experience by reducing stress, increasing comfort, and making the process more engaging. Attendees will learn how a first‑of‑its‑kind video game designed and developed by Rock Paper Reality specifically for blood donation, paired with an original soundtrack created in collaboration with the Chicago Symphony Orchestra, delivers calming, immersive support during the blood donation experience. The talk will also highlight the technical partnerships with Samsung, Google, and Qualcomm that enabled these experiences to run on the new Galaxy XR headset, making large‑scale deployment possible across dozens of countries. Perfect for innovators, healthcare leaders, and XR practitioners, this session showcases how immersive technology can drive meaningful change in donor motivation and public health.
sessions.next: How BCI is Removing the Friction from AR and AI Interaction

sessions.title: How BCI is Removing the Friction from AR and AI Interaction
sessions.speaker: Guy Wagner - Wearable Devices LTD
sessions.description: We are entering the era of "motionless interaction," where the boundary between thinking and doing begins to dissolve. In this session, we explore how Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI) are moving beyond the lab and onto the wrist to redefine our relationship with AR and AI. We will dive into the "Holy Grail" of neural control - a world where intention turns directly into action without the need for bulky headsets, cameras, or even physical movement. Discover the technical roadblocks facing the industry today, the critical importance of neural privacy, and how Mudra’s roadmap is pioneering a frictionless future for spatial computing.
sessions.next: XR UX for the Moment

sessions.title: XR UX for the Moment
sessions.speaker: Steven Wolpe - Pretty Big Monster
sessions.description: From social AR effects to fully immersive VR sessions, XR shows up in very different places. The defining factor isn’t the technology, it’s the user’s moment: how they enter, how much attention they have, what signals they can use, and what the environment allows. This session introduces an XR UX fit check built around Entry, Attention, Signals, and Environment. It offers a simple, repeatable way to catch UX mismatches early and design experiences that feel obvious, comfortable, and trustworthy.
sessions.next: Tangible Intangibles: How Biosignals Are Shaping the Future of Mixed Reality Art Therapy

sessions.title: Tangible Intangibles: How Biosignals Are Shaping the Future of Mixed Reality Art Therapy
sessions.speaker: Mahsa Nasri - Northeastern University
sessions.description: XR is becoming a new medium for mental health, not by replacing therapy, but by expanding what’s possible when we treat the body as an input and expression channel. This talk explores a design space at the intersection of physiological signals (especially eye tracking), AI/GenAI, and mixed reality, asking "What new forms of emotional reflection and art-making become possible when attention, emotional arousal, and regulation can be sensed in real time?" Mahsa introduces practical “building blocks” for translating signals like pupil dynamics and eye-movement patterns into visual, spatial, and material behaviors, and how GenAI can turn those behaviors into personalized metaphors, story fragments, and evolving aesthetic languages. Using MR artifacts as examples, we'll map the opportunity landscape from bio-responsive art prompts to persistent emotional sculptures and shared reflective spaces, along with the key design constraints (privacy, consent, misinterpretation, and avoiding “diagnosis UX”). Attendees will leave with concrete patterns for creating bio-expressive XR experiences that are inspiring, ethically grounded, and technically feasible.
sessions.next: The View In 2036: How New Headmounted Devices and Always-On AI Will Change the Way We See the World

sessions.title: The View In 2036: How New Headmounted Devices and Always-On AI Will Change the Way We See the World
sessions.speaker: Cortney Harding - Friends with Holograms
sessions.description: Ten years from now, we'll interact with the world via smartglasses that provide us with contextual and relevant inputs powered by AI. How can designers and developers start thinking about and building for this future to make sure it benefits humans? How can we make sure this technology is evenly distributed and not hamstrung by infrastructure failures? And in an "always-on" meta-physical world, how do we find time to disconnect and think?
sessions.next: From Scans to XR: A Practical Pipeline for Cultural Heritage

sessions.title: From Scans to XR: A Practical Pipeline for Cultural Heritage
sessions.speaker: Ines Said - Tanit XR; Margarita Johnson - Tanit XR
sessions.description: Join Ines Said for a high-level pipeline to turn real-world cultural heritage sites into immersive XR experiences. Discover how to use low-cost mobile tools like Scaniverse and Kiri Engine, when to use Gaussian splats vs photogrammetry meshes, and the key steps to optimize and deliver these assets into real-time, cross-platform XR applications. This session is for developers, creators, and anyone interested in learning how reality capture can become interactive XR content, and how community-driven efforts can make this work possible without requiring expensive equipment or studio-level budgets.
sessions.next: Building a Global Ecosystem for Physical AI

sessions.title: Building a Global Ecosystem for Physical AI
sessions.speaker: Ryan Wang - Outpost Capital; Michael Gold - Depth Map Ventures
sessions.description: Physical AI—intelligent systems that perceive, learn, and act in the real world—is moving rapidly from research labs into factories, cities, and homes. Yet unlike digital AI, Physical AI cannot scale through software alone. It requires a global ecosystem spanning hardware, data, simulation, deployment environments, and human collaboration. This talk examines what it truly takes to build a global Physical AI ecosystem, and why no single company, country, or platform can do it alone. We’ll explore how robotics, XR, simulation, cloud infrastructure, and local deployment partners must interconnect to enable Physical AI to scale safely and economically across regions. Drawing from real-world examples across the U.S., Asia, and emerging markets, the session highlights how ecosystems form around shared standards, developer tools, testbeds, and capital—and where today’s bottlenecks remain. The talk closes with a practical framework for founders, platform builders, and investors on how to participate in—and help shape—the global Physical AI ecosystem over the next decade.
sessions.next: When Less is More: Avoiding Unnecessary Geometric Constraints in XR Task Instructions

sessions.title: When Less is More: Avoiding Unnecessary Geometric Constraints in XR Task Instructions
sessions.speaker: Steve Feiner - Columbia University
sessions.description: When creating assembly instructions for 3D virtual objects in XR, users are often required to specify each object’s position and orientation (pose) relative to a parent coordinate system. However, there are some situations in which we might not care about the full pose of an object. And others in which only the relative relationship between a pair of objects might matter, rather than their relationships to other objects. Examples include providing instructions for another person or a robot system that has different constraints than we have and shouldn’t be restricted by our constraints. I will present an overview of approaches to address this that avoid specifying more information than is needed for a 3D task. And I will describe an XR user interface our lab is developing that supports mixing full specification of object poses for tasks that need it and only partial specification for tasks that don’t.
sessions.next: Optics Technologies Trends for the Next Generation of Smart Glasses

sessions.title: Optics Technologies Trends for the Next Generation of Smart Glasses
sessions.speaker: Thomas Bithell - IDTechEx
sessions.description: The optics used in augmented reality (AR) play a significant role in developing devices with acceptable weight, battery life and form factor. There have been several notable product announcements in the past 12 months, including Meta’s Ray Ban Display in September 2025, and there are expected releases from Snap, Google, and others in 2026. Across these developments, a consistent theme is an emphasis on eyewear that prioritizes conventional style and wearability, with technology integrated as subtly as possible. Waveguides play an important role in enabling this approach, supporting both the aesthetic requirements of fashionable glasses and the functional demands of AR display systems. There are a wide range of waveguide technologies and substrate options available supported by a diverse global ecosystem of suppliers. This talk will discuss how technology and material decisions are shaping the outlook for the adoption of different waveguide architectures and optical substrates in future smart glasses.

sessions.next: Socratic Style Debate on the Future of Immersive Tech

sessions.title: Socratic Style Debate on the Future of Immersive Tech
sessions.speaker: Alvin Graylin - Our Next Reality | Virtual World Society; Louis Rosenberg - Unanimous AI; Leslie Shannon - Virtual World Society; Kent Bye - Voices of VR Podcast
sessions.description: Four XR industry leaders with differing opinions on various topics related to immersive tech will participate in a Socratic Dialogue - a debate style that encourages critical thinking and an exploration of ideas. This unique type of session was first presented last year and encourages audience participation as everyone is encouraged to contemplate complex issues. sessions.next: PANEL: You’ve launched your game, now what? A talk about extending your game’s profitability.

sessions.title: PANEL: You’ve launched your game, now what? A talk about extending your game’s profitability.
sessions.speaker: Andy Fidel - Spatial Networks; David Wyatt; Jasmine Uniza - Impact Reality / Flat2VR Studios; Eric Masher - Impact Reality / Flat2VR Studios
sessions.description: At a time when most developers are concerned more with their existing apps’ viability than launching totally new experiences, there’s an imperative to promote games post-launch. Join this lively session with marketer/publishers Jasmine Uniza and Eric Masher from Impact Reality as well as David Wyatt, communications expert, most recently with Mighty Coconut building the Walkabout Mini Golf success story. You will learn what no/low-cost marketing tactics will keep players aware, returning, engaged, and supporting your studio financially.
sessions.next: Notable Lessons from Exploring AI Modalities in Higher Education

sessions.title: Notable Lessons from Exploring AI Modalities in Higher Education
sessions.speaker: Becky Lane - Cornell University
sessions.description: As artificial intelligence moves beyond the screen into wearables, audio agents, AR/VR, and ambient systems, higher education faces new pedagogical, ethical, and institutional questions. In this session, Dr. Becky Lane introduces Cornell University’s AI Modalities Initiative (AIM)—an interdisciplinary, evolving effort within the Center for Teaching Innovation that explores how AI modality, embodiment, and form factor shape learning and human experience. Drawing on early-stage pilots, student-led projects, exploratory industry partnerships, and emerging research, the talk focuses on what institutions are learning so far—including open questions, design challenges, and initial insights. Participants will gain a framework for approaching multimodal AI experimentation thoughtfully, responsibly, and in ways that can inform future teaching, research, and policy development.
sessions.next: No More Flat Sliders: Designing Interfaces in a 3D Reality

sessions.title: No More Flat Sliders: Designing Interfaces in a 3D Reality
sessions.speaker: Ophelia Chen - Dreamscape Learn
sessions.description: The leading platform for creating virtual reality (VR) interactions today is Unity, widely adopted for developing immersive experiences ranging from infotainment applications to games. While traditional user interface (UI) design principles for 2D mobile apps and websites provide a foundational reference, designing UI for VR demands an entirely distinct approach. This necessity arises from unique spatial considerations and limitations inherent to immersive environments — such as restricted fields of vision, depth perception challenges, ergonomic comfort, accessibility factors, and interaction precision. In this session, we will explore these specialized design principles and propose insights on developing a more effective and user-friendly VR UI library.
sessions.next: XR at Electric Utilities: Deployments, Lessons Learned, and the Path to Broader Adoption

sessions.title: XR at Electric Utilities: Deployments, Lessons Learned, and the Path to Broader Adoption
sessions.speaker: Julia Uhr - EPRI
sessions.description: Electric utilities have been slowly adopting XR technologies, and interest and investment are growing. The industry has a real need for immersive worker technologies, with an aging workforce, complex and hazardous equipment, and operational processes that are often outdated, and early results suggest XR can deliver real value across training, field operations, and maintenance. This talk will draw on case studies from EPRI's Digital Worker Collaborative to illustrate how utilities are beginning to use AR and VR across their field and plant operations, and what those deployments have revealed about where the technology has the most value. It will also address what needs to happen for the industry to move from isolated pilots to broader adoption, and offer a perspective on where utility XR applications are headed next.
sessions.next: OpenXR Deep Dive: From Runtime Architecture to Spatial Computing

sessions.title: OpenXR Deep Dive: From Runtime Architecture to Spatial Computing
sessions.speaker: Frédéric Plourde - Collabora and Khronos Group
sessions.description: OpenXR is the foundation for cross-platform XR, but real interoperability requires more than targeting a single API. As XR platforms diversify, developers are seeing that conformance, runtime behavior, and extension support all shape actual portability. OpenXR continues to evolve to address these challenges. This session explores the latest OpenXR developments shaping cross-platform XR in practice. We begin with the emerging spatial entities framework, including anchors, planes, markers, and persistence, enabling applications to understand and interact with shared spatial context across sessions and devices. These capabilities move OpenXR beyond hardware abstraction toward a common spatial layer for mixed reality.
sessions.next: The Known Unknowns: Storytelling at the Frontier of Immersive Film

sessions.title: The Known Unknowns: Storytelling at the Frontier of Immersive Film
sessions.speaker: Kathleen Cohen - The Collaboratorium; Elad Offer - Adorable Monsters; Emily Olman - Hopscotch Interactive
sessions.description: Director Elad Offer doesn't wait for a roadmap. Filming his latest project, an immersive dance film captured in both Blackmagic 180 Immersive VR and AINA's full volumetric technology, meant navigating a landscape of known (and unknown) unknowns at every turn. It is the kind of challenge he has made a habit of, most notably as the director of Bono: Stories of Surrender, the first ever feature-length 180 Immersive VR film released on Apple Vision Pro for Apple TV+. Joining him is Emily Olman, CEO of Hopscotch Interactive, AWE Advisory Council member, and producer on the dance film, who brings the view from the other side of the same production. Together, and in conversation with host Kathleen Cohen, Tech Humanist and XR Immersive Strategist, the three dig into what this production taught them, what surprised them, and what they would approach differently knowing what they know now. For anyone making immersive work at the edge of what is currently possible, this is the conversation you actually want to have.
